Request a Free Quote → or Email us →
— only page sitewide with ' or
findings.recommendation: Convert sustainability's banner to the .cta-email/.cta-banner-btn pattern and demote the email link to text style.

findings.title: val-card component typography forks by page: h3/DM Sans-700 vs h4/Cormorant-600
findings.evidence: CSS contains both `.val-card h3{font-family:var(--fb);font-size:1.15rem;font-weight:700;color:var(--navy);letter-spacing:-.01em}` and `.val-card h4{font-family:var(--fd);font-size:1.1rem;font-weight:600;color:var(--navy)}`. about.html renders val-cards with h3 (h4 count 0); accessibility.html (8x h4), services.html (16 h4), sitemap.html (7 h4), index-reference.html (7 h4) use h4 — the same card reads sans-bold on About and serif-regular on Accessibility/Services.
findings.recommendation: Pick one heading level (h3 fits both pages' outlines) and delete the orphan rule so the component renders identically everywhere.

findings.title: GTM, Clarity and HubSpot load pre-consent; consent banner only gates after the fact
findings.evidence: GTM (GTM-W2ZWXZ3T) and Clarity (w546w8zoh2) execute synchronously in <head> (lines 4-7 both pages); HubSpot loader //js-na2.hs-scripts.com/245755967.js is unconditional (sustainability:265, accessibility:191). cookie-consent.js only honors GPC and writes localStorage post-choice; banner text says 'By continuing, you agree...' after trackers already fired. cookie-consent.js also injects an inline style (<div style="display:flex;gap:.5rem">) violating the zero-inline-style rule.
findings.recommendation: Defer Clarity/HubSpot behind consent (GTM Consent Mode default-denied), and move the banner's inline flex style into the .cookie-banner CSS.

findings.title: Heading case drifts between Title Case and sentence case within and across both pages (brand = sentence case)
findings.evidence: sustainability.html mixes sentence-case h1/h2 ('The cleanest spaces in New York are also the safest.', 'When we say safe, we mean everyone.') with Title Case h2 '100% Eco-Certified. Every Product. Every Visit. Since Day One.' (86) and 'Third-Party Certified. Independently Verified.' (94). accessibility.html headings are mostly Title Case: 'Digital Accessibility for Everyone', 'Measures We Take', 'Conformance Status', 'Technologies Used', 'Known Limitations', 'Enforcement Procedure', 'Assessment and Review' — while its h1 is sentence case. Spaced em dashes: 8 occurrences on sustainability (4 in body copy, e.g. 'respiratory conditions — and', 'animals — no exceptions'), 7 on accessibility (e.g. 'everyone — regardless of ability', 'Third-party content — Some widgets') vs brand 'no em dashes'.
findings.recommendation: Normalize all headings to sentence case and replace spaced em dashes with periods/commas per PRODUCT.md voice.

findings.title: No <main> landmark on either page; skip-link targets a <section>
findings.evidence: grep '<main' → 0 in both files. <a href="#main" class="skip-link"> points to <section class="page-hero" id="main"> (sustainability:56, accessibility:54); all page content lives in sibling <section>/<div> elements with no main/contentinfo-complementary structure beyond nav and footer.
findings.recommendation: Wrap hero-through-CTA content in <main id="main"> on every page.

findings.title: Hero image preload inconsistent: sustainability preloads its LCP image, accessibility does not
findings.evidence: sustainability.html:29 <link rel="preload" href="images/stock/12-green-products.webp" as="image">; accessibility.html has no preload and its hero (images/stock/hero-office.webp, 87KB) is only discoverable via the inline background-image style at line 55 — late LCP discovery.
findings.recommendation: Add the same preload line for hero-office.webp.

findings.title: Minor ARIA/touch-target issues shared by both pages
findings.evidence:
(1) Breadcrumb separators / and footer-legal · are not aria-hidden — announced by screen readers. (2) cert-ico card 1 uses literal '✓' WITHOUT aria-hidden (sustainability:98) while cards 2-3 wrap SVGs in aria-hidden spans. (3) `.footer-social a` is 36x36px vs project 44px touch rule (`.nm-social a` is correctly 44px). (4) Newsletter honeypot:
wraps an — contradictory ARIA (hidden ancestor + labeled control).
findings.recommendation: aria-hidden the separators and the ✓ glyph, bump footer social targets to 44px, drop the honeypot aria-label.

findings.title: Anti-pattern inventory on these pages: side-stripes, hero metrics, animated gradient banner, glassmorphism nav
findings.evidence: `.problem-sec::before` = 4px vertical green gradient stripe (sustainability 'The Hidden Problem' section); `.eco-note{border-left:3px solid var(--green)}` (accessibility 'Feedback' box); `.hero-stats` metric rows on both heroes ('100% Eco-Certified / Zero VOCs / Plant-Based Products'; 'WCAG 2.1 AA / Keyboard Accessible / Screen Reader Ready'); `.cta-banner{background:linear-gradient(-45deg,#0b1d38,#1e3562,#0f2847,#162d52);background-size:300% 300%;animation:gradientShift 8s ease infinite}` on both (correctly disabled under prefers-reduced-motion); nav backdrop-filter glassmorphism. Gradient TEXT (.hero h1 em shimmer) exists in System A but does NOT apply here — sustainability's h1 safest. has no matching rule (.page-hero h1 em absent) and renders plain italic, a drift vs index's gradient em.
findings.recommendation: Decide per redesign direction: remove side-stripes and animated gradient, keep hero rows as plain text if retained, and either style or drop the h1 em device consistently.

findings.title: Misnamed/drifted components: .green-box renders navy, .benefit-num holds icons not numbers
findings.evidence: `.green-box{background:linear-gradient(135deg,var(--navy) 0,var(--navy-l) 100%)}` — the LEED callout on the sustainability page is navy despite the class name and the page's green theme. `.benefit-num{font-size:2rem}` was built for numeral glyphs but all five benefit cards now contain 22px SVGs (lines 178-194), leaving the rule inert. Sections at lines 154 and 172 also omit the .sec-lbl kicker used by the page's other four sections.
findings.recommendation: Rename or restyle green-box, rename benefit-num to benefit-ico (with cross-page grep first per CSS-purge lesson), and add kickers or accept the rhythm break deliberately.

findings.title: Accessibility statement freshness/completeness gaps
findings.evidence: 'This statement was last reviewed on April 1, 2026' (line 137) predates the June 2026 quote-form dark-mode overhaul (commits D125-D129). Feedback channel is email-only with no phone or postal address; enforcement section names DOJ but the statement omits the formal standards/technologies citation for the quote flow's separate stylesheet/dark theme.
findings.recommendation: Re-review after the quote-form changes land, add phone + address to the feedback block once canonical contact data is published.

findings.title: Visible breadcrumbs lack BreadcrumbList structured data sitewide
findings.evidence: Both pages render <nav aria-label="Breadcrumb"> trails; grep 'BreadcrumbList' across *.html and blog/*.html → 0 matches.
findings.recommendation: Emit BreadcrumbList JSON-LD matching the visible trail.

reports.inventory:
DESIGN-SYSTEM MEMBERSHIP: both pages 100% System A (styles.css v=15.1, Cormorant Garamond + DM Sans, navy/green tokens, cool cream #F3F5F8 sec-cream alternation); zero qf2/System B classes, no Fraunces/Caveat. Cache busters on both pages match site standard (styles.css?v=15.1, main.js?v=4.6, chat-widget.js?v=4.3, cookie-consent.js?v=1.1).
CLAIMS CENSUS (for cross-page synthesis): years — "over 12 years ago" (sustainability:87), "Over 12 years later" (about.html:79), "With over 12 years in the facility services industry, Alex" (about.html:152), "12+ Years" (about hero), "12+ years of excellence. Green Seal certified." (index.html footer only; the other 19 pages use the shorter "Premium eco-friendly janitorial and facility services across all 5 boroughs of New York City."); founding year "since 2014" appears ONLY in mockups.html. Eco: "100% Eco-Certified" (sustainability hero + h2), "Zero VOCs", "Plant-Based Products", "up to 90%" water reduction (sustainability:179, unique claim), LEED IEQ support (203), "Green Seal Certified" x8 sitewide vs "Green Seal Eco-Certified" x2 (why-ecco.html, index-reference.html); ISSA CIMS mentioned only in blog/choose-commercial-cleaning-company-nyc.html. Founders "Alex and Bianca" only on about.html + sustainability.html (consistent). Testimonial "Dr. Patricia Morales, Practice Owner, East Side Family Health": byte-identical quote on sustainability.html:211 and testimonials.html; index.html runs a TRUNCATED variant ("Our patients include children and elderly individuals with respiratory conditions..." — drops "people with sensitive skin") — altered quote of same named person. WCAG claims exist only on accessibility.html (3 mentions). Phone "929" → 0 occurrences sitewide; schema "telephone"/"streetAddress" → 0 occurrences; "addressLocality":"New York" → 21 pages. Dead g.co/kgs/eccofacilities → 53 occurrences / 26 files (HTTP 404 verified). Missing images/logo-vertical.png referenced by 24 files. robots.txt allows everything except /admin/ — dev artifacts (mockups.html, font-compare.html, font-specimen.html, color-swatches.html, index-reference.html, quote.backup.html, mobile-test.html, emailjs-template.html) crawlable. sitemap.xml: 24 URLs, includes both audited pages. Inline styles: sustainability 5, accessibility 2. Heading censuses — sustainability: 1 h1, 7 h2, 16 h3, 0 h4 (order valid); accessibility: 1 h1, 5 h2, 6 h3, 8 h4 (h2→h4 skip at lines 88→93). prefers-reduced-motion: global kill-switch in styles.css covers orbs/pulse/gradientShift — the page's "Reduced Motion" claim is accurate (verified, no finding).

reports.name: pag-blog-index
reports.summary: Blog area (blog.html + 7 posts; deep-audited 5-signs, janitorial-vs-day-porter, eco-certified) is pure System A (navy/Cormorant/DM Sans, orb hero, animated navy-gradient CTA banner, glassmorphism nav) with zero System B presence, but its copy violates the new brand direction wholesale (Title Case, 37 em dashes across the 4 pages). The biggest defects are infrastructure rot: every blog page references images/logo-vertical.png which DOES NOT EXIST (favicon + og:image + twitter:image + BlogPosting image = broken social cards and 404 favicons on all 7 posts); all 7 posts put the WHITE logo variant on the always-near-white glassmorphism nav, rendering the header logo invisible; and the related-articles component on all 7 posts plus the card date/link classes on blog.html reference CSS classes that have zero rules in css/styles.css (the stylesheet defines a replacement component, .more-articles-grid/.more-articles-card, that no HTML uses) — the classic cross-page purge trap. One post (janitorial-vs-day-porter) has contradictory publish dates (visible Feb 28, 2026 vs structured-data 2026-03-22). Schema LocalBusiness on all pages lacks telephone, street address, and sameAs, and no canonical phone (929) 280-9374 appears anywhere in page content. Blog cards otherwise match posts (6/7 exact titles, 7/7 dates vs visible meta, all links resolve); the 7-post template is internally consistent (same author line, date format, CTA pattern; no reading time or <time> elements anywhere).

findings.title: All blog og:image/twitter:image/favicon/BlogPosting image point to nonexistent images/logo-vertical.png
findings.evidence: `ls images/logo-vertical.png` → "No such file or directory". Referenced 4x in each of the 7 posts (link rel="icon" href="../images/logo-vertical.png"; og:image, twitter:image, and JSON-LD BlogPosting "image" all = https://eccofacilities.com/images/logo-vertical.png) and 2x in blog.html (og:image, twitter:image) = 30 refs in this area; 23 HTML files reference it site-wide. Every social share of every article renders no image (with twitter:card summary_large_image, which expects a wide image, not a vertical logo even if it existed), and every post tab 404s its favicon. blog.html itself uses the correct favicon-32/16.png pair — posts diverge.
findings.location: blog.html lines 19,24; blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html lines 21,26,27,32; blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html lines 21,26,27,32; blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html lines 21,26,27,32; identical pattern in the other 4 posts
findings.recommendation: Replace with an existing asset: favicon → ../images/favicon-32.png/16.png (match blog.html); og:image/twitter:image/BlogPosting image → each post's actual hero image (e.g. images/stock/22-handshake.webp) or images/logo-horizontal.png as fallback. Sweep all 23 referencing files.

findings.title: White logo on near-white glassmorphism nav — header logo invisible on all 7 blog posts
findings.evidence: All 7 posts: `` while ALL 15 root pages use images/logo-horizontal.png (dark). css/styles.css: `.nav{...background:rgba(250,251,252,.92);backdrop-filter:blur(20px) saturate(1.2)...}` — the nav background is always near-white (#FAFBFC at 92%); `.nav.scrolled` only adds box-shadow, and styles.css has 0 occurrences of prefers-color-scheme, so there is no dark-nav state. logo-horizontal-white.png is the variant used on the navy footer.
findings.location: blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html line 41; blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html line 41; blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html line 41; same in the other 4 posts (nav-logo img src)
findings.recommendation: Change nav-logo src to ../images/logo-horizontal.png in all 7 posts to match the root pages.

findings.title: Related-articles component on all 7 posts uses CSS classes that no longer exist; stylesheet defines a replacement component no HTML uses
findings.evidence:
All 7 posts contain `
` with `` → `
` + h3 + `
Read More →
` (2 links/post, 14 total). grep counts in css/styles.css: .article-links=0, .article-link=0, .article-link-date=0, .article-link-arrow=0. Instead CSS defines .more-articles-grid{display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr}, .more-articles-card{padding:1.5rem;border:1px solid var(--bl);border-radius:var(--r)}, .more-articles-card h4, .more-articles-card span — used by 0 HTML files. Rendered result: no grid/cards/hover; article titles are forced into `.more-articles h3{font-size:1rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.1em}` (identical to the "More Articles" label), dates and "Read More →" fall back to the global anchor rules (a{text-decoration:none;color:inherit} at byte 1193, overridden by a{color:var(--blue-l)} at byte 11028). Git -S finds no commit where these classes existed in styles.css. This matches the project's known cross-page CSS purge trap (MEMORY.md).
findings.location: blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html lines 99-113; blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html lines 98-112; blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html lines 94-108; + same block in the other 4 posts; css/styles.css .more-articles* rules
findings.recommendation: Either migrate all 7 posts' markup to .more-articles-grid/.more-articles-card (h4 + span per the CSS contract) or restore the .article-link* rules. One source of truth; verify hover/touch targets after.

findings.title: janitorial-vs-day-porter publish date contradicts itself: visible Feb 28 vs structured data Mar 22
findings.evidence: Visible: article-meta `February 28, 2026` (line 67), blog.html card date "February 28, 2026" (line 108), related-links on 5-signs (line 108) and eco-certified (line 103) both say "February 28, 2026". Structured: `article:published_time content="2026-03-22"` (line 18) and JSON-LD `"datePublished":"2026-03-22"` (line 32). The other 6 posts' meta dates all match their visible dates exactly. Google can display the 2026-03-22 date in SERPs while the page shows Feb 28.
findings.location: blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html lines 18,32 vs line 67; blog.html line 108
findings.recommendation: Pick one date (visible Feb 28 is the one echoed in 3 other places) and align article:published_time + datePublished to 2026-02-28.

findings.title: LocalBusiness JSON-LD on all blog pages is incomplete vs canonical identity: no telephone, no street address, no sameAs; phone absent from page content entirely
findings.evidence: Identical block on blog.html line 31 and all 7 posts line 31: `"address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"New York","addressRegion":"NY","addressCountry":"US"}` — no streetAddress/postalCode (canonical: 54 State St #804, Albany NY 12207), no telephone (canonical: (929) 280-9374), no sameAs despite LinkedIn/Instagram/Google links in nav+footer. grep 'tel:\|(929)\|929-280\|Albany\|54 State' across blog.html + blog/*.html → 0 hits; footer Contact column offers only info@eccofacilities.com and "New York City — All 5 Boroughs". Name "Ecco Facilities LLC" and email are correct.
findings.location: blog.html line 31; blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html line 31; blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html line 31; blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html line 31 (+ other 4 posts)
findings.recommendation: Add telephone "+19292809374", full PostalAddress, and sameAs array to the shared LocalBusiness block (site-wide template change); consider exposing the phone in the footer Contact column.

findings.title: blog.html card internals unstyled: .blog-card-date and .blog-card-link have zero CSS rules
findings.evidence:
7 cards each use `
` and `Read Article →`. css/styles.css occurrence counts: .blog-card-date=0, .blog-card-link=0 (while .blog-card, .blog-card h3, .blog-card p are styled). Dates render at full body size/color instead of the small muted treatment used by every styled date elsewhere (.more-articles-card span{font-size:.75rem;color:var(--tm)}); the CTA link relies on the global a{color:var(--blue-l)} with no underline, no margin separation, and no 44px touch target (project rule: min 44px with display:inline-flex).
findings.recommendation: Add .blog-card-date and .blog-card-link rules (or rename to existing styled classes); give the link a ≥44px inline-flex touch target.

findings.title: blog.html heading order skips h1→h3; the page's only h2 is the CTA banner after the article list
findings.evidence: Heading inventory: 1 h1 ("Insights for Better Facilities"), 11 h3, 1 h2. DOM order: h1 → 7× card h3 ("How to Choose the Right Commercial Cleaning Company in NYC" etc.) → h2 "Have a question about your facility? We're here to help." → 4× footer h3. The #articles section has no h2 landmark naming the list. Posts are fine (h1 → h2 sections → h3).
findings.recommendation: Add a (visually hidden if desired) h2 like "Latest articles" on the #articles section, or demote card headings consistently.

findings.title: 7 identical 'Read Article →' link texts on blog.html; card titles are not part of the link
findings.evidence:
Each card: `

title

...Read Article →` — the anchor text is identical 7 times (WCAG 2.4.4 link purpose; screen-reader link list is 7× "Read Article"). Contrast: post related-links wrap the whole card incl. the h3 in the anchor (good). Bonus inconsistency: cards 1-2 use the entity `→`, cards 3-7 use literal `→` — evidence of two authoring passes.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the whole card in the anchor (matching the post pattern) or add aria-label/visually-hidden title text to each link; normalize arrow encoding.

findings.title: Blog copy violates brand direction: Title Case everywhere and 37 em dashes across the 4 audited pages
findings.evidence: Em-dash counts (— char incl. '— Ecco Facilities' title separators): blog.html=5, 5-signs=8, janitorial-vs-day-porter=15, eco-certified=9. Body examples: "that's not a sign of quality — it's a sign of volume" (5-signs line 78); "Your facility needs to be clean — that's not the question" (janitorial line 69); "This isn't a minor side effect — it's a health problem you're creating" (eco line 77); card copy "taking shortcuts with your facility — and what it means for your business" (blog.html line 98). All headings/cards/CTAs are Title Case ("Insights for Better Facilities", "The Key Difference: Scheduled vs. Real-Time") vs PRODUCT.md sentence-case direction. Per-direction this is System A legacy voice; flagged for cross-system reconciliation.
findings.location: blog.html, blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html, blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html, blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html (site copy throughout)
findings.recommendation: Decide whether brand direction (sentence case, no em dashes) applies to the marketing site; if yes, batch-edit blog titles/copy — note title-tag em dashes also affect SERP display.

findings.title: System A anti-pattern stack on blog pages: animated gradient CTA banner, pulse-dot hero badge, blurred orbs, 100vh hero on a listing page, keyword 'hero-stats'
findings.evidence: .cta-banner{background:linear-gradient(-45deg,#0b1d38,#1e3562,#0f2847,#162d52);background-size:300% 300%;animation:gradientShift 8s ease infinite} on all 4 pages; .pd{...animation:pulse 2.5s infinite} in blog.html hero-badge "Ecco Blog · Insights & Tips"; 4 .orb divs (filter:blur(80px), orbFloat 20s) per hero; blog.html uses full .page-hero{min-height:100vh} pushing all 7 articles below the fold; .hero-stats component filled with keywords, not stats: `Expert AdviceFacility ManagementHealthier Workspaces` (line 64). Nav glassmorphism backdrop-filter:blur(20px) saturate(1.2) on all pages. All conflict with the Editorial/Warm/Adult, no-gradient direction; zero System B (sage/cream/Fraunces) presence in this area.
findings.location: blog.html lines 56-66, 116-121; cta-banner + page-hero-short on all 3 posts; css/styles.css .cta-banner/.orb/.pd/.page-hero
findings.recommendation: For synthesis: blog area is wholly System A; if redesign proceeds, the CTA banner gradient animation and orb/pulse decorations are the highest-visibility tokens to retire; replace blog hero-stats keywords with real stats or remove.

findings.title: Inline style attributes: 12 across the 4 pages (project rule is zero)
findings.evidence: blog.html (3): hero `style="background-image:url('images/stock/23-coworking-space.webp')"`, section `style="background:var(--wh)"` (load-bearing — .sec has no background rule), GTM noscript iframe `style="display:none;visibility:hidden"`. Each post (3 ea.): image wrapper `style="margin-bottom:2.5rem;border-radius:var(--rl);overflow:hidden"`, ``, + GTM iframe. Same pattern in the other 4 posts (total 24 in blog area).
findings.location: blog.html lines 35, 57, 69; blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html lines 36, 68; blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html lines 36, 68; blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html lines 36, 68
findings.recommendation: Create .article-hero-img component classes (base+mobile+desktop per project rules); keep GTM iframe as documented boilerplate exception or class it.

findings.title: Nav dropdown ARIA misuse (site-wide, present on all 4 pages)
findings.evidence: `<a href="services.html" class="nl" aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded="false">` — a link that both navigates and claims popup semantics; aria-expanded is static in markup; dropdown uses role="menu"/role="menuitem" (application-menu pattern) for plain navigation links. Project rule requires viewport-aware ARIA via matchMedia; the desktop hover dropdown vs mobile drawer share the same static attributes.
findings.location: blog.html line 43; blog/*.html line 44 (identical nav partial on every page)
findings.recommendation: Drop role=menu/menuitem (use plain list semantics), toggle aria-expanded in JS per viewport, or split trigger from link. Fix once in the shared nav partial.

findings.title: Analytics fire before consent; script loading inconsistent between index and posts
findings.evidence: GTM (GTM-W2ZWXZ3T) and MS Clarity (w546w8zoh2) execute synchronously in <head> on all 4 pages while cookie-consent.js?v=1.1 loads at the end of <body> — consent cannot gate them; HubSpot (245755967.js) also loads unconditionally. Loading drift: posts use `<script src="../js/main.js?v=4.6" defer>` and chat-widget defer, blog.html (and index/about/services) load the same scripts without defer.
findings.recommendation: Gate GTM/Clarity/HubSpot behind the consent state (or document why not); normalize defer usage across all pages.

findings.title: blog.html card for dirty-office post truncates the title ('…Impacts Productivity' vs h1 '…Impacts Productivity in NYC')
findings.evidence: Card h3 (blog.html line 91): "The Hidden Cost of a Dirty Office: How Cleanliness Impacts Productivity"; post h1/title: "The Hidden Cost of a Dirty Office: How Cleanliness Impacts Productivity in NYC". The other 6 cards match their posts' h1 verbatim; all 7 card links resolve to existing files; all 7 card dates match the posts' visible article-meta dates; cards are text-only (no thumbnails), so no image mismatches possible.
findings.recommendation: Align the card title with the post h1 (or vice versa).

findings.title: Title suffix and date semantics inconsistent: 'Ecco Facilities LLC' vs 'Ecco Facilities'; no <time> elements; sitemap lastmod drift
findings.evidence:
blog.html title: "Blog — Ecco Facilities LLC"; all 7 post titles end "— Ecco Facilities" (no LLC). 0 elements across all 8 blog files — dates live in plain divs (`
By the Ecco Facilities Team · March 15, 2026
`). sitemap.xml lastmod for the 3 older posts = 2026-04-03 while their dateModified meta = 2026-03-29. No BreadcrumbList schema despite visible breadcrumbs; blog.html has no Blog/ItemList schema.
findings.location: blog.html line 13; blog/*.html line 13; sitemap.xml blog entries
findings.recommendation: Standardize one brand suffix; wrap dates in <time datetime>; regenerate sitemap lastmod from page dateModified.

findings.title: Posts lack fonts.gstatic.com preconnect and lazy-load their near-fold hero image
findings.evidence: blog.html has ``; all 7 posts have only the fonts.googleapis.com preconnect (grep fonts.gstatic.com: blog.html=1, every post=0). Post hero `` sits directly under the short hero — an LCP candidate lazy-loaded; no width/height attrs (height fixed at 300px via inline style, so CLS contained).

findings.title: Stock imagery weakly matched to topics; unsourced and over-promising claims in eco post
findings.evidence: 5-signs hero: 22-handshake.webp alt "Professional business handshake" for an article about cleaning corner-cutting; janitorial-vs-day-porter hero: generically named 4.webp alt "Modern office workspace". eco-certified line 76: "Studies show that workers in facilities cleaned with harsh chemicals take more sick days" — no source; line 78: "Your turnover decreases. Your sick days drop." — flat guarantees; line 89: "every single product we use is Green Seal certified" — consistent with why-ecco.html ("100% Green Seal certified, non-toxic") but stronger than index/sustainability phrasing ("100% eco-certified"); keep claims aligned site-wide.
findings.location: blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html line 68; blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html line 68; blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html lines 76, 78, 89
findings.recommendation: Swap heroes for topic-relevant images; cite or soften the studies/outcome claims; standardize the certification claim wording across pages.

reports.inventory: PAGES: blog.html (168 lines) + 7 posts (162-204 lines), all System A, styles.css?v=15.1 (site-wide consistent; only dev artifact quote.backup.html uses v=13.7). CARD↔POST MATRIX (card date / meta-visible date / published_time): choose-commercial Apr 5 / Apr 5 / 2026-04-05 OK; benefits-day-porter Apr 5 / Apr 5 / 2026-04-05 OK; checklist Apr 4 / Apr 4 / 2026-04-04 OK; dirty-office Apr 4 / Apr 4 / 2026-04-04 OK (title truncated on card); 5-signs Mar 15 / Mar 15 / 2026-03-15 OK; eco-certified Mar 8 / Mar 8 / 2026-03-08 OK; janitorial-vs-day-porter Feb 28 / Feb 28 / 2026-03-22 MISMATCH. dateModified: older 3 posts 2026-03-29, newer 4 = published date; sitemap lastmod 2026-04-03/04/05. TEMPLATE (identical across 7 posts): author line "By the Ecco Facilities Team · DATE"; inline-styled 300px hero img; no reading time anywhere (0 hits "min read"); no tags; no ; More Articles = .article-links/.article-link (0 CSS rules, all 7 posts); per-post CTA banner h2 + "Request a Quote →"; scripts with defer (index without). ORPHANED CLASSES (0 rules in styles.css): article-links, article-link, article-link-date, article-link-arrow, blog-card-date, blog-card-link. UNUSED CSS COMPONENT (0 HTML consumers): more-articles-grid, more-articles-card (+h4/span). MISSING ASSET: images/logo-vertical.png (30 refs in blog area, 23 files site-wide). LOGO VARIANTS: nav root pages=logo-horizontal.png, all 7 posts=logo-horizontal-white.png (invisible on rgba(250,251,252,.92) nav). EM DASH COUNTS: blog.html 5, 5-signs 8, janitorial 15, eco 9 (incl. 3 title-tag separators each). INLINE STYLES: 3/page (24 total blog area). IDs per page unique (nav, navMenu, mobTog, main, articles, btt, hs-script-loader). Trackers: GTM-W2ZWXZ3T, Clarity w546w8zoh2, HubSpot 245755967, Mailchimp us22 list — consistent across area. Schema: LocalBusiness (no telephone/streetAddress/sameAs) + BlogPosting (author=Organization, image=missing logo-vertical.png) on posts; blog.html LocalBusiness only.

reports.name: pag-blog-posts
reports.summary: The four blog posts are pure System A pages (styles.css v15.1, Cormorant Garamond/DM Sans, navy orb hero, cta-banner) with consistent nav/footer chrome and matching cache-buster versions, but they carry two broken-asset defects: the day-porter post's hero image src points to a non-existent file (gyms.webp; actual asset is "Gyms & Fitness.webp"), and all four reference images/logo-vertical.png (favicon + og:image + twitter:image + BlogPosting.image, 4 refs per page, 24 pages site-wide) which does not exist anywhere in the repo or git history. Schema LocalBusiness on every post omits telephone and street address entirely and says addressLocality "New York" vs the canonical 54 State St #804, Albany NY 12207. Template drift is real but contained: the choose- post swaps the cta-banner/more-articles order and uses unique CTA copy, in-article closing patterns vary 4 ways (no promo / plain promo / bold CTA), contextual internal links range 1-5 per post, and the "More Articles" recommendation network gives the three newest posts zero inbound sibling links. Bylines, date formats, and schema dates are internally consistent across my four posts (no future dates); the only date conflict is in the cited sibling janitorial-vs-day-porter (byline Feb 28 vs schema Mar 22), which two of my pages propagate. Voice runs against the PRODUCT.md editorial direction: every h2 is Title Case and em dashes appear in all titles/descriptions (16 head-metadata occurrences + 4 footer —), though body copy is clean of em dashes and reads adult/non-urgent.

findings.title: Broken hero image: gyms.webp does not exist (day-porter post)
findings.evidence: src="../images/stock/gyms.webp" — no such file. images/stock/ contains "Gyms & Fitness.webp" (capitalized, with spaces) but no gyms.webp. grep across all *.html: this is the only reference site-wide. Cloudflare Pages is case/name-sensitive, so the article hero renders as a broken 300px-tall image with alt text "High-traffic commercial building lobby". Even the nearest existing asset is a gym/fitness photo, off-topic for a commercial-lobby day porter article, and would contradict the alt text.
findings.location: blog/benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html line 68 (img inside article hero div, directly under the byline)
findings.recommendation: Point src at an existing, topically correct asset (e.g. a lobby/common-area photo such as images/stock/hero-office.webp or Corporate Offices.webp), keep alt accurate to the chosen image, and rename assets to lowercase-hyphenated names.

findings.title: images/logo-vertical.png missing from repo but used as favicon, og:image, twitter:image and BlogPosting.image on all 4 posts (24 pages site-wide)
findings.evidence: find . -iname '*logo-vertical*' returns nothing; git ls-files has no record (never tracked, not an uncommitted deletion). Each of the 4 posts references it exactly 4 times: <link rel="icon" type="image/png" href="../images/logo-vertical.png">, og:image, twitter:image (with twitter:card summary_large_image), and BlogPosting "image". 24 HTML files site-wide reference it, including index.html og:image. Meanwhile root pages (index.html, blog.html) already migrated favicons to images/favicon-32.png / favicon-16.png (both exist) — blog posts still carry the stale single icon link. Result: 404 favicon on every post, blank social-share cards, invalid BlogPosting image for rich results.
findings.location: blog/choose-commercial-cleaning-company-nyc.html lines 21,26,27,32; blog/commercial-cleaning-checklist-nyc.html lines 21,26,27,32; blog/dirty-office-costs-productivity.html lines 21,26,27,32; blog/benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html lines 21,26,27,32; plus 20 more pages incl. index.html
findings.recommendation: Replace favicon links with the favicon-32/16 pair used by index.html and blog.html; set og:image/twitter:image/BlogPosting.image to each post's actual hero photo (1200x630-ish), not a logo.

findings.title: Schema.org LocalBusiness: no telephone, no streetAddress/postalCode, locality conflicts with canonical Albany address
findings.evidence: Identical block on all 4 posts: "address":{"@type":"PostalAddress","addressLocality":"New York","addressRegion":"NY","addressCountry":"US"} — missing streetAddress and postalCode entirely; no "telephone" field anywhere in the JSON-LD. Canonical identity: 54 State St #804, Albany NY 12207, phone (929) 280-9374. The visible page also shows no phone anywhere (0 matches for tel:/929 in all 4 posts; footer contact column has email only).
findings.recommendation: Insert canonical streetAddress "54 State St #804", addressLocality "Albany", postalCode "12207" and telephone "(929) 280-9374" into the LocalBusiness block (single shared snippet), and decide whether the footer should expose the phone.

findings.title: Template drift: end-block order swapped in choose- post (cta-banner before more-articles)
findings.evidence: choose-commercial-cleaning-company-nyc.html: <div class="cta-banner"> at line 137, <div class="more-articles"> at line 144 (CTA first). The other 3 posts: more-articles first, then cta-banner (checklist 111/127, dirty-office 103/119, benefits 132/148). CTA copy also drifts: choose uses "Ready to find the right cleaning partner for your facility?" + "Request a Free Walkthrough →" while the other 3 share identical "Ready for a cleaning company that does it right?" + "Request a Quote →".
findings.location: blog/choose-commercial-cleaning-company-nyc.html lines 137-158 vs blog/commercial-cleaning-checklist-nyc.html lines 111-132, blog/dirty-office-costs-productivity.html lines 103-124, blog/benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html lines 132-153
findings.recommendation: Standardize post template order (article → more-articles → cta-banner) and pick one CTA heading/button pattern, allowing only the button label to vary contextually.

findings.title: In-article closing CTA pattern varies 4 ways; contextual internal links range 1 to 5 per post
findings.evidence: choose: NO closing promo, zero "Ecco" mentions in body, 4 contextual links (sustainability, janitorial, day-porter, why-ecco). checklist: promo paragraph "At Ecco Facilities, we build customized cleaning programs…" + 1 contextual link total (quote.html). dirty-office: promo paragraph + 1 link total (quote.html). benefits: 5 links (day-porter, janitorial, testimonials, services, quote) + bold standalone CTA line "Ready to see what a day porter can do for your building?". Four different end-of-article conventions across four posts.
findings.recommendation: Define one closing convention (e.g. 2-3 contextual links per post + a single plain promo paragraph) and retrofit; checklist and dirty-office especially need contextual links to service pages.

findings.title: Stale "More Articles" network: the 3 newest posts receive zero inbound links from sibling posts
findings.evidence: Inbound sibling-post link counts: choose-commercial-cleaning-company-nyc.html ← 0, dirty-office-costs-productivity.html ← 0, benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html ← 0; vs 5-signs ← 4, janitorial-vs-day-porter ← 4, checklist ← 3, eco-certified ← 3. All recommendation slots in all 7 posts point at the older March/February posts; nothing was updated when the April 4-5 posts shipped. The 3 newest posts are reachable only from blog.html.
findings.location: more-articles blocks: blog/choose-commercial-cleaning-company-nyc.html lines 144-158; blog/commercial-cleaning-checklist-nyc.html lines 111-125; blog/dirty-office-costs-productivity.html lines 103-117; blog/benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html lines 132-146 (and the 3 older posts' blocks)
findings.recommendation: Rebalance More Articles so each post gets at least 1-2 inbound recommendations; obvious pairs: benefits-day-porter ↔ janitorial-vs-day-porter, choose ↔ 5-signs, dirty-office ↔ checklist.

findings.title: Inline styles: 3 per post, 12 total (project rule is zero)
findings.evidence: Each post contains exactly 3 style= attributes: style="margin-bottom:2.5rem;border-radius:var(--rl);overflow:hidden" (hero figure div) and style="width:100%;height:300px;object-fit:cover" (hero img), both on line 68 of every post, plus GTM noscript boilerplate style="display:none;visibility:hidden" (line 36). The two hero-figure styles are author-added and identical across all 4 posts — clearly a missing .article-hero-img component class.
findings.recommendation: Create an .article-hero-img class in styles.css (base+mobile+desktop) and replace the 8 author-added inline styles; GTM noscript style is vendor boilerplate and can be exempted explicitly.

findings.title: Cited sibling post has conflicting dates: "February 28, 2026" citations vs schema 2026-03-22
findings.evidence:
checklist and benefits posts both render
February 28, 2026
for janitorial-vs-day-porter.html. That target post's own byline says "February 28, 2026" but its BlogPosting schema says "datePublished":"2026-03-22" — a 22-day internal conflict. My pages propagate the byline side; whichever is corrected, these two citation blocks (and blog.html, which also shows February 28) must follow. No future dates anywhere; all 7 posts' dates precede today (2026-06-10).
findings.location: blog/commercial-cleaning-checklist-nyc.html line 120; blog/benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html line 141; root defect in blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html (byline vs line with datePublished)
findings.recommendation: Fix the source post's schema/byline mismatch first, then sweep all article-link-date citations (2 in my pages + blog.html) to match.

findings.title: Voice drift vs PRODUCT.md: Title Case headings throughout and em dashes in all titles/descriptions
findings.evidence: Every h2 in all 4 posts is Title Case, e.g. "Verify Insurance and Bonding", "Look for Transparent, Flat-Rate Pricing", "Daily Cleaning Tasks", "Sick Days Add Up Fast", "Clutter and Dirt Kill Focus", "Real-Time Response to Spills and Messes", "Get a Day Porter for Your Building" (33 h2s total) — brand direction is sentence case. Em dashes: choose post has 7 in head metadata ("…in NYC — Ecco Facilities" in title/og/twitter; "…signing a contract — from certifications to pricing transparency." in description/og/twitter/schema); the other 3 posts have 3 each (title pattern); plus "New York City — All 5 Boroughs" in every footer (4 occurrences). Body paragraphs are clean — zero em dashes in article copy across all 4 posts.
findings.location: All h2 elements and head metadata lines 13-32 in the 4 post files; footer contact column (line 177/151/143/172 respectively)
findings.recommendation: Decide whether the editorial sentence-case/no-em-dash direction applies to System A marketing pages; if yes, retitle h2s to sentence case and swap em dashes for periods/commas in titles and meta descriptions (keep "— Ecco Facilities" only if adopted as the official title-separator convention site-wide).

findings.title: CTA promise/destination mismatch: "day porter quote form" links to generic quote.html; over-promise close
findings.evidence: benefits post: "Fill out our day porter quote form" — quote.html is the generic V2 wizard, not a day-porter form. A dedicated quote-dayporter.html exists but is orphaned (linked only from quote-janitorial.html and itself). Same paragraph closes with "have your porter on-site faster than you expect" — unverifiable speed promise, urgency-adjacent copy the brand direction prohibits.
findings.recommendation: Either relabel to "request a quote" or deep-link the wizard with day-porter preselected; rewrite the closing promise to something verifiable (e.g. "we typically respond within one business day" if true and Alex-confirmed).

findings.title: Five different quote-CTA phrasings on each single post page
findings.evidence: On every post, the same quote.html destination is labeled: "Get a Free Quote" (nav), "Request a Quote →" or "Request a Free Walkthrough →" (cta-banner), "Get Your Free Proposal →" (footer-cta), "Request a Quote" (footer contact column), and in-article "reach out for a free quote"/"request a free quote"/"Fill out our day porter quote form". 5 distinct labels per page for one action.
findings.location: All 4 posts: nav line 50, cta-banner block, footer-cta (footer top), footer contact column, in-article closing links
findings.recommendation: Pick one primary verb pair (e.g. "Get a free quote" / "Request a walkthrough") and apply across nav, banners, and footers site-wide.

findings.title: Duplicate hero image across two cross-linked posts, with divergent alt text for the same file
findings.evidence: choose post and checklist post (published one day apart, each recommending the other or appearing together in More Articles) both use ../images/stock/Corporate Offices.webp as hero; alts differ for the identical image: "NYC office building lobby" (choose) vs "Modern corporate office space" (checklist). Also unencoded spaces in src URLs ("Corporate Offices.webp", "Coworking Spaces.webp") — technically invalid attribute URLs browsers must auto-encode — and asset naming is inconsistent (lowercase "gyms.webp" referenced vs Title-Case-with-spaces real files).
findings.recommendation: Give each post a distinct hero; normalize stock filenames to lowercase-hyphenated and update references.

findings.title: Publication date not machine-readable; heading-level wart in More Articles block
findings.evidence:
Byline is
By the Ecco Facilities Team · April 5, 2026
— no element (all 4 posts). og article:published_time uses date-only "2026-04-05" rather than full ISO 8601 datetime. In more-articles, the block label

More Articles

and each card title

inside the sit at the same heading level (3 sibling h3s), flattening the outline. Otherwise heading structure is clean: exactly one h1 per page, all sections h2, no skips.
findings.location: All 4 posts: line 67 (byline), line 18 (og meta), more-articles blocks (choose 144-158, checklist 111-125, dirty 103-117, benefits 132-146)
findings.recommendation: Wrap dates in <time datetime="2026-04-05">, use full ISO datetimes in OG meta, demote card titles to h4 or styled non-headings.

findings.title: Unattributed or loosely attributed statistics in dirty-office and checklist posts
findings.evidence: dirty-office: "average office desk harbors roughly 400 times more bacteria than a toilet seat" (no source; the underlying U. of Arizona study is uncredited); "Studies from the Harvard Business Review suggest … up to 5% more productive" (HBR is a magazine, not a research body); "delivers a return of three to five times the investment"; "$2,000 to $5,000 per month" typical NYC cleaning cost (public pricing anchor synthesis should cross-check against the quote wizard). checklist: "Dirty light fixtures can reduce lighting output by up to 30 percent" (no source); "Hard floors … need to be stripped … and re-waxed every quarter" (aggressive vs industry norm of 1-2x/year, and strip/wax is positioned elsewhere as Ecco project work). Properly attributed: CDC $10.4B flu cost, Princeton Neuroscience Institute clutter research, EPA 2-5x indoor air pollution, WGBC 8-11% productivity.
findings.recommendation: Attribute or soften the unsourced figures; have Alex confirm the $2,000-$5,000/month public price anchor and the quarterly strip/wax cadence claim.

findings.title: Title and meta-description length overruns
findings.evidence: dirty-office <title> is 97 chars ("The Hidden Cost of a Dirty Office: How Cleanliness Impacts Productivity in NYC — Ecco Facilities") — will truncate in SERPs (~60 char display). Meta descriptions: checklist 181 chars, choose 169 chars (both over the ~160 display budget); dirty-office 172 chars; benefits 173 chars. All four descriptions exceed 160.
findings.location: blog/dirty-office-costs-productivity.html line 13; meta description line 14 in all 4 post files
findings.recommendation: Trim titles to <60 chars before the brand suffix and descriptions to 150-160 chars.

findings.title: Breadcrumb truncation inconsistent across the 4 posts
findings.evidence: dirty-office breadcrumb final crumb is shortened to "The Hidden Cost of a Dirty Office" while its h1 is the full "…: How Cleanliness Impacts Productivity in NYC"; the other 3 posts repeat the full h1 text verbatim in the breadcrumb. No documented truncation rule.
findings.location: blog/dirty-office-costs-productivity.html line 60 vs line 61; compare line 60 in the other 3 posts
findings.recommendation: Pick one rule (always shorten long titles, or always mirror h1) and apply to all posts.

findings.title: meta charset declared after two inline tracking scripts; legacy script patterns
findings.evidence: <meta charset="UTF-8"> sits at line 8 after the GTM (~480 bytes) and Clarity (~330 bytes) inline scripts — close to the 1024-byte limit within which charset must appear; best practice is first element in head. Also: HubSpot loader uses protocol-relative src "//js-na2.hs-scripts.com/245755967.js" (legacy pattern) and cookie-consent.js?v=1.1 is the only local script loaded without defer. Identical on all 4 posts (and site-wide).
findings.recommendation: Move charset to the first head element, use https:// for HubSpot, add defer to cookie-consent.js if its execution order allows.

findings.title: Design-system membership: all 4 posts are clean System A; decorative orb hero is the only flagged pattern
findings.evidence:
All 4 load only css/styles.css?v=15.1 + Google Fonts (DM Sans + Cormorant Garamond); zero qf2/System B classes, no Fraunces/Caveat, no gradient text, no border-left side-stripes, no hero metrics in the posts themselves. Each hero contains
x4 — the System A decorative-orb signature (glassmorphism-adjacent), aria-hidden not set on the decorative container. Script/CSS versions match the site standard (main.js?v=4.6, chat-widget.js?v=4.3, cookie-consent.js?v=1.1); no cache-buster drift in these 4 files.
findings.location: All 4 posts: line 30 (stylesheet), line 58 (hero-bg orbs), lines 198-200 area (scripts)
findings.recommendation: No migration needed for these pages; when System A/B reconciliation happens, posts inherit whatever the marketing shell decides; consider aria-hidden="true" on .hero-bg.

[2] commercial-cleaning-checklist-nyc.html: 2026-04-04 (match), hero "Corporate Offices.webp" (duplicate of [1]) alt "Modern corporate office space", 1 contextual link (quote), promo close, recommends 5-signs + janitorial-vs (cites Feb 28), CTA "Request a Quote". [3] dirty-office-costs-productivity.html: 2026-04-04 (match), hero "Coworking Spaces.webp", 1 link (quote), promo close, recommends checklist + eco-certified, breadcrumb truncated, title 97 chars. [4] benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html: 2026-04-05 (match), hero src gyms.webp = 404 (real file "Gyms & Fitness.webp"), 5 links, bold CTA close mislabeled "day porter quote form"→quote.html, recommends checklist + janitorial-vs (cites Feb 28). SHARED: identical nav/footer/GTM-W2ZWXZ3T/Clarity w546w8zoh2/HubSpot 245755967; styles.css?v=15.1, main.js?v=4.6, chat-widget.js?v=4.3, cookie-consent.js?v=1.1 (site-consistent; only quote.backup.html drifts at v13.7/v4.4); favicon = missing logo-vertical.png (root pages use favicon-32/16); LocalBusiness schema: name "Ecco Facilities LLC", email info@, address {locality New York, NY, US} — NO street/zip/telephone; BlogPosting author=Organization vs visible byline "By the Ecco Facilities Team"; 3 inline styles per page (12 total); one h1 per page; footer "© 2026 Ecco Facilities LLC", "New York City — All 5 Boroughs", email-only contact (no phone anywhere). STATS FOR CROSS-COMPARE: desk 400x bacteria vs toilet seat (unsourced); CDC flu $10.4B; NYC loaded cost $350-500/employee/day; HBR "up to 5%" productivity; $80k avg salary / 50-person office / $200k recovered; avoidable losses $125k-250k/yr; cleaning price anchor $2,000-5,000/month = $24k-60k/yr; ROI 3-5x; EPA indoor air 2-5x dirtier; WGBC 8-11%; light fixtures -30% output; strip/wax "every quarter"; slip-and-fall "tens of thousands"; restroom gap "eight-to-ten-hour". SIBLING DATES (for synthesis): 5-signs 2026-03-15 (byline=schema), eco-certified 2026-03-08 (match), janitorial-vs-day-porter CONFLICT byline Feb 28 vs schema 2026-03-22; blog.html lists Feb 28. Sitemap.xml includes all 7 post URLs. Inbound sibling links: choose 0, dirty 0, benefits 0, checklist 3, 5-signs 4, janitorial-vs 4, eco-certified 3.

reports.name: pag-legal-util
reports.summary: The four utility pages (privacy, terms, sitemap, 404) are pure System A (navy hero + orbs + animated-gradient CTA banner + Cormorant/DM Sans, consistent v=15.1 cache-busters, valid minimal JSON-LD, no duplicate IDs, all internal links resolve). The headline defect is legal: the Privacy Policy states the site deploys no third-party tracking/analytics cookies while every page (including privacy.html itself) loads GTM, Microsoft Clarity session recording, HubSpot tracking, and GA4 dataLayer events, and the cookie banner's Accept/Decline buttons gate none of them — making the hero's "CCPA Compliant" badge unsubstantiated. Both legal pages omit the company's real address and phone (canonical: 54 State St #804, Albany NY 12207, (929) 280-9374), giving only "New York City, NY". sitemap.html is stale (3 of 7 blog posts listed) while claiming "Always Updated / 20+ Pages / 6 Sections", and 404.html lacks the noindex the task probes for while carrying a self-referential canonical. Site-wide, og:image/twitter:image point to images/logo-vertical.png which does not exist (62 refs across 24 files), and privacy/terms use an orphan .cta-banner-actions class with zero CSS rules.

findings.title: Privacy Policy §4 falsely states no third-party tracking while GTM, Clarity, HubSpot and GA4 run on every page including the policy page itself
findings.evidence: privacy.html line 128: "Ecco Facilities does not use cookies for advertising or behavioral tracking purposes... We do not deploy third-party tracking cookies, analytics pixels, or retargeting technologies." Same file loads: GTM 'GTM-W2ZWXZ3T' (lines 4-5 + noscript iframe line 35), Microsoft Clarity 'w546w8zoh2' session-recording (lines 6-7), HubSpot tracker '//js-na2.hs-scripts.com/245755967.js' (line 231). js/main.js lines 5-7 push GA4 events: "/* GA4 dataLayer event tracking — fires when GTM is active */". The site's own cookie banner (js/cookie-consent.js line 12) admits: "We use cookies to improve your experience and analyze site traffic." _headers CSP whitelists googletagmanager.com, google-analytics.com, clarity.ms, js-na2.hs-scripts.com. Policy also fails to disclose HubSpot, GTM/GA4, Clarity, and the Mailchimp newsletter processor (form posts to eccofacilities.us22.list-manage.com on all pages); §3 discloses only the chat backend (which is accurate: js/chat-widget.js posts to https://ecco-chat-backend.vercel.app/api/chat).
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/privacy.html lines 4-7, 128, 230-232; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/js/cookie-consent.js line 12; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/js/main.js lines 5-7; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/_headers line 8 (tracking stack loads on ALL ~26 public pages)
findings.recommendation: Rewrite §4 to truthfully disclose GTM/GA4, Microsoft Clarity (session recording/heatmaps), HubSpot, and Mailchimp with purposes and opt-out; or actually remove those trackers. Add a third-party-processors list to §3.

findings.title: Cookie consent banner is decorative: Decline/GPC do not stop GTM or Clarity, which execute in <head> before any consent
findings.evidence: GTM and Clarity run synchronously in <head> on page load (privacy.html/terms.html/sitemap.html/404.html lines 4-8) regardless of stored consent. js/cookie-consent.js declineCookies() (lines 25-32) only sets localStorage 'ecco_cookies/ecco_consent' and pushes HubSpot _hsq 'doNotTrack'; the GPC branch (lines 2-8) likewise only gates HubSpot. grep of js/*.js shows the ONLY consumer of the consent keys is js/quote-flow.js lines 525-540 (PII persistence) — no consent-mode dataLayer push, no Clarity/GTM gating anywhere. So a user who clicks Decline keeps being tracked by GTM/GA4 and Clarity session recording.
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/js/cookie-consent.js lines 2-8, 25-32; head of all 4 audited pages (privacy.html, terms.html, sitemap.html, 404.html); /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/js/quote-flow.js lines 525-540
findings.recommendation: Implement real gating: load GTM/Clarity only after accept (or use Google Consent Mode defaults=denied), and honor GPC/decline for all trackers, not just HubSpot.

findings.title: "CCPA Compliant" hero badge on Privacy page is an unsubstantiated compliance claim
findings.evidence: privacy.html line 64 hero-stats: Your Data ProtectedCCPA CompliantNo Data Selling. The policy body never mentions CCPA, California, categories of personal information collected/disclosed, or a "Do Not Sell or Share" mechanism; §7 only says "Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the right to...". Combined with non-functional consent (see P0), the public claim is false. js/cookie-consent.js line 2 comments "CCPA 2026: Respect Global Privacy Control" but only gates HubSpot.
findings.recommendation: Remove the "CCPA Compliant" badge or add a genuine CCPA section (categories, rights, Do Not Sell/Share link, GPC honoring) backed by working consent gating.

findings.title: og:image/twitter:image reference non-existent images/logo-vertical.png (62 refs across 24 files site-wide; 6 on audited pages)
findings.evidence: find images -iname "*vertical*" returns nothing (images/ contains logo-horizontal.png, logo-horizontal-white.png, logo-footer.png only). privacy.html lines 19,24; terms.html lines 19,24; sitemap.html lines 20,25 all set https://eccofacilities.com/images/logo-vertical.png. Site-wide grep: 62 occurrences across 24 HTML files (every core page ×2, every blog post ×4, index.html ×3). Social shares of every page render with a broken/missing preview image.
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/privacy.html:19,24; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/terms.html:19,24; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/sitemap.html:20,25; plus 21 more HTML files
findings.recommendation: Add images/logo-vertical.png (1200x630 recommended) or repoint all og:image/twitter:image to an existing asset; prefer a real social card over a logo.

findings.title: sitemap.html directory is stale and its hero stats are false: lists 3 of 7 blog posts while claiming "20+ Pages / 6 Sections / Always Updated"
findings.evidence: Resources card (lines 101-108) links only blog/5-signs-cleaning-company.html, blog/eco-certified-cleaning-matters.html, blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html. Missing 4 published posts that exist on disk and in sitemap.xml since 2026-04-04/05: choose-commercial-cleaning-company-nyc.html, benefits-day-porter-high-traffic-buildings.html, commercial-cleaning-checklist-nyc.html, dirty-office-costs-productivity.html. Hero (line 64) claims "20+ Pages" but the directory lists 19 links = 17 unique pages; "6 Sections" matches nothing on the page (4 directory cards + 1 legal group = 5); "Always Updated" is contradicted by the 4 missing posts (~2 months stale). It does list only real pages (no dev artifacts) — that half passes.
findings.recommendation: Add the 4 missing blog posts; correct or remove the "20+ Pages / 6 Sections / Always Updated" hero stats; consider generating this page from sitemap.xml to prevent future drift.

findings.title: Legal contact blocks omit street address and phone; "New York City, NY" conflicts with canonical Albany registered address
findings.evidence: privacy.html lines 171-175 and terms.html lines 199-203 (both under heading "Contact Us"): "Ecco Facilities LLC / Email: info@eccofacilities.com / New York City, NY" — no street address, no phone. Canonical identity: 54 State St #804, Albany NY 12207, (929) 280-9374. grep '929' across all 4 pages: 0 occurrences. A privacy policy offering rights requests (§7) and a ToS specifying NY County venue (§11) should carry a mailing address; legal name "Ecco Facilities LLC" itself is used correctly throughout (privacy.html:95,172; terms.html:97,102,200; footers © 2026 Ecco Facilities LLC).
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/privacy.html lines 171-175 (§11 Contact Us); /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/terms.html lines 199-203 (§13 Contact Us)
findings.recommendation: Add the registered mailing address (54 State St #804, Albany NY 12207) and phone (929) 280-9374 to both contact sections; keep NYC as service area, not as the legal address.

findings.title: 404.html lacks noindex and carries a self-referential canonical; social meta block is partial
findings.evidence: 404.html has NO <meta name="robots"> (grep confirms zero robots meta on all 4 pages) and line 16 sets <link rel="canonical" href="https://eccofacilities.com/404.html">. A direct request to /404.html returns HTTP 200 on Cloudflare Pages, so the error page is crawlable and canonical-confirmed. Meta block has og:title only (line 15) — missing og:description, og:url, og:image, twitter:* present on every other page; no JSON-LD (acceptable). Nav and both CTAs work (index.html, quote.html exist); footer link set is complete.
findings.recommendation: Add <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> and drop the canonical; optionally complete the og/twitter block for consistency.

findings.title: Orphan class .cta-banner-actions has zero CSS rules — privacy/terms CTA banner uses different, unstyled markup vs sitemap's styled .cta-banner-btn
findings.evidence: grep '\.cta-banner-actions' css/styles.css = 0 matches; the full .cta-banner rule set contains only .cta-banner, ::before/::after, h2, p, and .cta-banner-btn{position:relative;z-index:2;margin-top:1.5rem}. Yet <div class="cta-banner-actions"> wraps the banner buttons on 11 pages: privacy.html:183, terms.html:211, about.html, blog.html, and all 7 blog posts. sitemap.html:155 instead uses <div class="cta-banner-btn">. Result: two competing markups for the same System A banner; on the 11 pages the button row has no flex/gap/top-margin styling and buttons sit at z-index auto under the ::before/::after orbs.
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/privacy.html:183; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/terms.html:211; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/sitemap.html:155; css/styles.css (.cta-banner rules); +8 more pages
findings.recommendation: Pick one wrapper class, add a real rule (flex, gap, margin-top, z-index:2), and normalize all 12 usages.

findings.title: No <main> landmark on any of the 4 pages; skip-link targets the decorative hero section
findings.evidence:
grep 'Skip to main content) points to
— the hero with background photo, orbs and badges — not the content. Content sections are bare
/
children of .
findings.recommendation: Wrap page content in <main id="main"> (hero can stay inside or above it) so the skip link and screen-reader landmark navigation land on real content.

findings.title: Heading-level skips on all 4 pages (h1→h3 on legal pages, h2→h4 ×7 on sitemap, h1→h3 on 404)
findings.evidence: privacy.html and terms.html: h1 (line 62) → h3 "Table of Contents" (line 78) before the first h2 "1. Information We Collect"/"1. Services Overview". sitemap.html: h2 "Everything in One Place" (line 75) → h4 cards "Services","Company","Resources","Get Started" (lines 82,91,101,112) and h2 "Policies & Legal" (line 126) → h4 "Privacy Policy","Terms of Service","Accessibility" (lines 132,138,144) — no h3 anywhere in main content. 404.html: h1 "Page Not Found" (line 49) → footer h3s with no h2 on the page.
findings.recommendation: Demote the TOC heading to h2 (or aria-labelled nav), promote sitemap card headings to h3, and keep one logical level per step.

findings.title: Hero pseudo-metrics (trust-badge spans) on legal/utility pages, two of nine values false
findings.evidence: hero-stats spans: privacy.html:64 "Your Data Protected · CCPA Compliant · No Data Selling"; terms.html:64 "Transparent Terms · Satisfaction Guarantee · Fully Insured"; sitemap.html:64 "20+ Pages · 6 Sections · Always Updated". "CCPA Compliant" and "Always Updated" are demonstrably false (see P1 findings); the rest are marketing assertions styled as metrics (.hero-stats, rgba(255,255,255,.6), 0.8rem/600) inside the System A hero-metrics pattern. Also full marketing hero treatment (background photo via inline style, 4 animated .orb divs, uppercase .hero-badge pill) on Privacy/Terms — pages where users come to read a document.
findings.recommendation: Strip hero-stats from legal pages (or replace with verifiable facts: effective date, jurisdiction); tone legal heroes down to a simple title block.

findings.title: 7 inline style attributes across the 4 pages plus a JS-injected inline style on every page — project rule is zero
findings.evidence: privacy.html:35,57; terms.html:35,57; sitemap.html:36,57; 404.html:25. Two patterns: GTM noscript iframe style="display:none;visibility:hidden" (4×) and hero-img style="background-image:url('images/stock/…webp')" (3×). Additionally js/cookie-consent.js line 12 injects <div style="display:flex;gap:.5rem"> into the cookie banner on all ~26 pages.
findings.recommendation: Move hero images to per-page CSS classes or a CSS custom property; add a .cookie-actions class; GTM iframe style is vendor boilerplate — acceptable exception or replicate via class.

findings.title: sitemap.xml lists two URLs that 301-redirect (legacy quote pages) and all lastmod stamps predate the latest content changes
findings.evidence: sitemap.xml lines 93-104 include https://eccofacilities.com/quote-janitorial.html and /quote-dayporter.html at priority 0.8, but _redirects lines 12-15 send both (with and without .html) 301 → /quote.html?service=… (on Cloudflare Pages _redirects takes precedence over existing static files; the _redirects comment at lines 8-9 even says "Do NOT add redirects for pages that already exist as .html files" and then does exactly that). Newest lastmod anywhere is 2026-04-18 while every core page was modified 2026-05-02 and quote.html 2026-05-02+. The ?service= targets are functional (js/quote-flow.js handles 'janitorial'/'dayporter', 53 'dayporter' refs).
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/sitemap.xml lines 93-104, all lastmod fields; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/_redirects lines 4-15
findings.recommendation: Remove the two redirecting URLs from sitemap.xml, refresh lastmod values, and resolve the _redirects self-contradiction (delete the legacy .html files or drop the redirects).

findings.title: 8 dev artifacts are deployed, crawlable and indexable (robots.txt disallows only /admin/)
findings.evidence: Publicly served with no noindex and no robots.txt disallow: mockups.html, font-compare.html, font-specimen.html, color-swatches.html, index-reference.html, quote.backup.html, mobile-test.html, emailjs-template.html. robots.txt (3 lines) disallows only /admin/; _headers adds X-Robots-Tag noindex only for /crm/*. quote.backup.html still references styles.css?v=13.7 and main.js?v=4.4 (the only version drift found site-wide — all 25 real pages use v=15.1/v=4.6). sitemap.html and sitemap.xml correctly omit the artifacts.
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/robots.txt; /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/_headers; artifact files at repo root
findings.recommendation: Delete the artifacts from the deploy or exclude them via robots.txt Disallow + X-Robots-Tag noindex in _headers.

findings.title: Four different CTA labels for the same quote.html destination on a single page
findings.evidence: On sitemap.html alone: nav "Get a Free Quote" (line 49), hero "Get a Quote →" (line 67), cta-banner "Request a Free Quote →" (line 155), footer-cta "Get Your Free Proposal →" (line 161). privacy.html and terms.html each carry 3 of these variants. Quote vs Proposal terminology also alternates (footer "Get Your Free Proposal" vs footer-col "Request a Quote").
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/sitemap.html:49,67,155,161; same nav/footer block on privacy.html, terms.html, 404.html (site-wide shared component)
findings.recommendation: Standardize on one primary CTA label (e.g. "Get a free quote") across nav, banner and footer.

findings.title: Hero badge pill text contrast computes to ~4.17:1 at 11.2px/600 — below AA, and varies over the hero photo
findings.evidence: .hero-badge: font-size .7rem (11.2px), weight 600, uppercase, color var(--green-l) #3D9A43 on background rgba(45,122,50,.15) over navy #0B1D38 → effective bg ≈ rgb(16,43,55); contrast(#3D9A43 vs rgb(16,43,55)) ≈ 4.17:1 < 4.5:1 required for non-large text. Because the pill is semi-transparent over a photo (privacy/terms: 22-handshake.webp; sitemap: hero-office.webp), real contrast is content-dependent and can be worse. Affected badge texts: "Your Privacy Matters", "Service Agreement", "Complete Site Directory" (component is site-wide System A).
findings.recommendation: Raise badge text to a lighter green or white, or solidify the pill background; verify with a contrast tool against the photo backgrounds.

findings.title: Meta http-equiv X-Frame-Options / X-Content-Type-Options are invalid as <meta> and ignored by browsers
findings.evidence: All 4 pages lines 10-11: <meta http-equiv="X-Content-Type-Options" content="nosniff"> and <meta http-equiv="X-Frame-Options" content="SAMEORIGIN">. Per spec these directives only work as HTTP response headers; _headers lines 2-3 already sets both correctly, so the meta tags are dead weight implying false security.
findings.recommendation: Delete the two meta tags on all pages; rely on _headers.

findings.title: Brand-voice drift: em dashes and Title Case in visible copy; h1 style split reveals System A/B drift
findings.evidence: Em dashes (PRODUCT.md: none): sitemap.html:63 hero sub "…quotes, and more — all in one organized directory"; footer "New York City — All 5 Boroughs" on all 4 pages; <title>/og:title use "—" separator on all 4 (raw counts: privacy 3, terms 3, sitemap 5, 404 2, + 1 — each). Title Case (rule: sentence case): hero badges "Your Privacy Matters"/"Service Agreement"/"Complete Site Directory", sec-ttl "Everything in One Place", h1s "Page Not Found"/"Privacy Policy"/"Terms of Service" — while sitemap.html's h1 "Find exactly what you're looking for." already uses the newer sentence-case editorial style, so the four pages mix both conventions.
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/sitemap.html:62-64,75; privacy.html:61-62; terms.html:61-62; 404.html:49; footers of all 4
findings.recommendation: Convert badges/headings to sentence case and replace visible em dashes per brand direction (title-tag separator is a defensible exception — decide once and document).

findings.title: Decorative hero photos exposed to screen readers via role=img; privacy and terms share the identical handshake image
findings.evidence: <div class="hero-img" style="background-image:url('images/stock/22-handshake.webp')" role="img" aria-label="Professional handshake"> appears on BOTH privacy.html:57 and terms.html:57 (same image, same label, both also <link rel=preload> it at line 29); sitemap.html:57 same pattern with hero-office.webp "Modern office building". These are decorative backdrops behind text and should be aria-hidden, not announced. Also footer-legal links are 0.75rem (12px) with no padding (touch-target suspects <44px), and .footer-legal span{display:none} hides the "·" separator markup that all 4 pages still ship.
findings.recommendation: Mark hero-img divs aria-hidden="true" (drop role/aria-label); give privacy or terms a distinct image; add padding to footer legal links.

findings.title: No BreadcrumbList JSON-LD despite visible breadcrumbs; page schema is minimal but clean (no stale address here)
findings.evidence: privacy.html, terms.html, sitemap.html each render a breadcrumb nav (line 60) but grep BreadcrumbList = 0 on all 4 pages. The single WebPage JSON-LD per page (line 31/32) is valid JSON with publisher {"@type":"Organization","name":"Ecco Facilities LLC","url":"https://eccofacilities.com"} — notably it does NOT carry the stale/malformed address+phone reported on other pages, because it omits address/phone entirely. 404.html has no JSON-LD (acceptable).
findings.recommendation: Add BreadcrumbList schema to the three breadcrumbed pages; when the canonical Organization schema is fixed site-wide, reference it via @id rather than duplicating.

findings.title: sitemap.html categorization oddities: "Home" filed under "Get Started", query-param quote variants listed as separate pages
findings.evidence: Get Started card (lines 112-118, heading "Get Started") lists "Free Quote" (quote.html), "Janitorial Quote" (quote.html?service=janitorial), "Day Porter Quote" (quote.html?service=dayporter) — three entries for one page — plus "Home" (index.html) as the fourth get-started action. Resources card mixes Testimonials with blog posts. All links resolve (params handled by js/quote-flow.js), so functional but structurally misleading for a directory claiming to map the site.
findings.recommendation: Move Home out of Get Started (or drop it — logo/nav already covers it) and label the quote variants as one entry, or keep variants but mark them as pre-filled flows.

findings.title: All 4 pages are pure System A with heavy decorative treatment on utility pages; no dark-mode support while quote.html has Editorial Midnight
findings.evidence: Pages use only System A tokens/components: navy page-hero + 4 .orb divs (404 included), cool-cream .sec-cream (#F3F5F8) on sitemap, Cormorant Garamond/DM Sans, and the animated-gradient .cta-banner (background:linear-gradient(-45deg,#0b1d38,#1e3562,#0f2847,#162d52); background-size:300% 300%; animation:gradientShift 8s ease infinite) on privacy/terms/sitemap. No System B (sage/Fraunces) leakage — internally consistent — but none of the 4 pages respond to prefers-color-scheme while quote.html ships a full dark variant, so navigating quote → privacy (linked from the quote form's consent copy) jolts from Editorial Midnight to bright navy/white.
findings.location: /Users/alexmercedes/Downloads/Ecco Webside/privacy.html, terms.html, sitemap.html, 404.html (whole pages); css/styles.css .cta-banner/.orb rules
findings.recommendation: For synthesis: decide whether utility/legal pages adopt System B's quieter editorial style (they are the most likely pages reached FROM the quote flow); at minimum tame the animated gradient banner and orbs on legal pages.

reports.inventory:
TRACKING STACK (loads on all 4 pages + site-wide): GTM container GTM-W2ZWXZ3T (head, all pages); Microsoft Clarity w546w8zoh2 (head, all pages); HubSpot 245755967 via //js-na2.hs-scripts.com (body end, all pages); GA4 events via js/main.js dataLayer pushes; Mailchimp footer form -> eccofacilities.us22.list-manage.com (u=0c07ef5e654e35834b79d8ac7, id=ebe6d4e045); chat backend https://ecco-chat-backend.vercel.app/api/chat (matches privacy §3 disclosure — only accurate third-party disclosure in the policy). CSP (_headers) whitelists all of the above.
PAGE CENSUS: 23 real public pages (index, about, services, janitorial, day-porter, why-ecco, testimonials, careers, sustainability, blog + 7 posts, accessibility, privacy, terms, sitemap, 404, quote, quote-janitorial*, quote-dayporter*; *=301 via _redirects) + 8 deployed dev artifacts (mockups, font-compare, font-specimen, color-swatches, index-reference, quote.backup, mobile-test, emailjs-template). sitemap.html lists 19 links = 17 unique pages (missing 4 blog posts); sitemap.xml lists 23 URLs incl. the 2 redirecting legacy quote pages; newest lastmod 2026-04-18 vs files modified 2026-05-02.
VERSIONS (consistent on all 25 real pages): styles.css?v=15.1, main.js?v=4.6, chat-widget.js?v=4.3, cookie-consent.js?v=1.1; only quote.backup.html drifts (v=13.7 / v=4.4); quote.html intentionally omits chat-widget.js (D125).
LEGAL FACTS FOR CROSS-PAGE COMPARISON (terms.html): effective date March 29, 2026 (same on privacy.html); governing law State of New York, venue New York County (§11); satisfaction guarantee = notify within 24 hours, re-clean at no charge (§4); cancellation = no long-term lock-in, 30 days written notice, 24h notice for single-visit cancellation (§5); liability cap = total fees paid for the specific service, property-damage claims within 48 hours (§7); proposals valid 30 days (§2); \"fully insured and bonded\", \"all team members background-checked\" (§8). Footer tagline on all 4: \"Premium eco-friendly janitorial and facility services across all 5 boroughs of New York City.\" Copyright © 2026 Ecco Facilities LLC.
CLEAN CHECKS (verified, no findings): no duplicate IDs on any of the 4 pages; exactly one h1 each; all internal hrefs resolve to existing files (incl. blog post links and accessibility.html); TOC anchors #section-1..11 (privacy) and #section-1..13 (terms) all exist; hero images 22-handshake.webp / hero-office.webp exist; .btt back-to-top is 44x44px; quote.html?service= params handled by quote-flow.js; legal-* CSS classes all defined except .cta-banner-actions; JSON-LD parses as valid JSON on the 3 pages that have it; footer-legal link contrast computes ~4.83:1 (passes AA); legal name \"Ecco Facilities LLC\" used correctly everywhere on these 4 pages.

reports.name: performance
reports.summary: Performance posture is split exactly along the two design systems: quote.html (System B) got real optimization passes (async font loading with noscript fallback, idle-deferred GTM/Clarity, deferred quote-flow.js, responsive AVIF/WebP avatars, documented preload removals) while the 25 System A marketing pages run parse-time GTM+Clarity in head, render-blocking font CSS, and no defer on main.js/chat-widget.js (blog posts alone use defer). The homepage references ~4.0MB of images, 2.62MB of it six unoptimized JPEGs loaded eagerly as inline-style CSS backgrounds that can never lazy-load, while fetchpriority="high" is wasted on the 180x40 nav logo. The worst single defect is sitewide: og:image/twitter:image/schema point to images/logo-vertical.png which does not exist (62 refs, 24 pages). All first-party JS ships unminified (quote-flow.js is 265KB with 955 comment lines), styles.css is ~26% dead rules sitewide and =69% unused on quote.html where it stacks with the 210KB quote-flow.css. Asset hygiene: ~10.4MB of deployed-but-unreferenced images (28 of 56 stock files, the entire images/spaces dir, the 585KB original alina-avatar.jpg, two PNGs totaling 5.2MB), plus tracked dev artifacts (index-reference.html, mobile-test.html, emailjs-template.html, two *.original.js). Two orchestrator premises did not verify: the quote.html font link is NOT duplicated (second link is a noscript fallback), and quote.backup.html / quote-flow.source.css / quote-flow.backup.js / mockups / font-*.html / color-swatches.html are gitignored+untracked and therefore NOT deployed; deployed cache-buster versions are internally consistent per file.

findings.title: Sitewide og:image/twitter:image/schema.org image points to nonexistent images/logo-vertical.png
findings.evidence: 62 occurrences across 24 pages reference https://eccofacilities.com/images/logo-vertical.png; `ls images/logo-vertical.png` => No such file or directory, and it is not in git. index.html line 19 (og:image), line 24 (twitter:image), line 43 (schema.org "image"). Every social share / link unfurl for every page renders with a broken or missing preview image.
findings.location: All 24 public pages incl. index.html:19,24,43, about.html:19,24, blog.html, careers.html, day-porter.html, janitorial.html, services.html, sitemap.html, privacy.html, accessibility.html, plus blog/*.html and index-reference.html:12
findings.recommendation: Either add a real images/logo-vertical.png (1200x630 social card preferred) or point all og:image/twitter:image/schema refs at an existing asset; do it via one sweep since the URL is identical on every page.

findings.title: Homepage ships 2.62MB of unoptimized JPEG via inline-style CSS backgrounds; total image references ~4.0MB
findings.evidence: index.html photo-card-bg divs use style="background-image: url(...)" pointing at raw JPGs: 'Safe for sensitivities.jpg' 853,220B, 'Residential Buildings.jpg' 564,909B, 'Safe for children.jpg' 523,481B, 'Restaurants.jpg' 255,966B, 'Safe for your team.jpg' 218,863B, 'Safe for the environment.jpg' 202,067B (e.g. index.html:329,345,400). Sum of all images referenced by index.html = 4,001,233B. CSS background images cannot take loading="lazy" or fetchpriority, so all load eagerly. WebP siblings exist for some (e.g. 8-nyc-skyline-night has both) but the JPG is what's referenced; 6 of these have no WebP/AVIF variant in use.
findings.recommendation: Convert the six JPG card backgrounds to WebP/AVIF (target <120KB each), or better, replace photo-card-bg divs with inside the cards (also fixes the zero-inline-styles project rule violation and enables native lazy loading).

findings.title: All first-party JS ships unminified; quote-flow.js is 264,947B with 955 comment lines
findings.evidence: js/quote-flow.js starts with a banner comment block ('Quote Flow v2.0 — Flow-based navigation architecture'), grep -c '^\s*//' = 955 comment lines; raw 264,947B / gzip 73,552B. js/chat-widget.js (56,016B) and js/main.js (21,083B) are also readable source with full identifiers. Only vendor files (chart.min.js, sortable.min.js) are minified. Project conventions mandate CSS minification but JS has no minification step at all.
findings.location: js/quote-flow.js (loaded by quote.html ?v=38.0), js/chat-widget.js (24 pages, ?v=4.3), js/main.js (25 pages, ?v=4.6)
findings.recommendation: Add a JS minify step (esbuild/terser) to the same pipeline that runs clean-css; quote-flow.js alone should drop from ~73KB to roughly 35-45KB gzipped and parse much faster on mobile.

findings.title: styles.css is ~26% dead rules sitewide (43.4KB of 169.9KB)
findings.evidence: styles.css defines 661 unique class names across 1,706 rules; 203 classes have zero references in any deployed HTML or JS (197 after excluding the anim-* set built dynamically at js/main.js:339 `layer.className='word-layer anim-'+def.anim`). 402 of 1,706 rules reference only dead classes = 43,433B = 25.6% of the file. Dead clusters map to retired features: ask-pill* (13 classes, Ask Alina stub retired in D125), alina-corner* (4), inline-followup* (5), feat-test* (7), hero-morph*, day-grid/day-btn-abbr, continue-btn/continue-spinner.
