findings.location: css/styles.css (minified, single line); haystack = all 25 deployed pages + js/main.js, js/chat-widget.js, js/cookie-consent.js, js/quote-flow.js
findings.recommendation: Purge with an AST-based tool (per project memory: never regex on the minified file, grep ALL *.html first, keep anim-* and other dynamic-concat classes); expect ~43KB raw / ~8-10KB gzip savings on every page load.

findings.title: quote.html double-stylesheet payload: 380KB raw CSS for one page, styles.css =69% unused there
findings.evidence: quote.html loads css/styles.css?v=15.1 (169,918B raw / 30,807B gz) plus css/quote-flow.css?v=50.3 (210,488B raw / 35,532B gz). Generous attribution (counting any rule whose selector shares = 1 class with quote.html markup/JS, plus all element-only rules) marks at most 52,974B of styles.css's 168,215 rule bytes (31%) as potentially used — it is needed only for nav/footer/newsletter chrome (108 non-qf classes: nav, nm-*, ndd, footer-*, skip-link...). Total quote.html first-party transfer ˜167KB gzip / 760KB uncompressed (html 20,328gz + styles 30,807 + quote-flow.css 35,532 + quote-flow.js 73,552 + main.js 6,066 + cookie-consent ~800).
findings.location: quote.html (link tags ~line 58-59; scripts lines 1186, 1194, 1195); css/styles.css; css/quote-flow.css
findings.recommendation: Extract the nav/footer subset of styles.css into a small shared chrome stylesheet (or inline it into quote-flow.css build), dropping ~115KB raw / ~21KB gz from the highest-intent conversion page.

findings.title: Analytics bootstrap is parse-time-blocking on 25 pages but idle-deferred only on quote.html
findings.evidence: index.html lines 5 and 7 run GTM (GTM-W2ZWXZ3T) and Clarity (w546w8zoh2) loaders synchronously during head parse; identical pattern on all 15 marketing pages, 7 blog posts, quote-janitorial.html, quote-dayporter.html (clarity=1 gtm=2 on all 26 checked files). quote.html instead wraps both in window load + requestIdleCallback({timeout:2500}) 'deferred until idle to avoid blocking LCP' (quote.html lines 4-7). The inline loader scripts themselves execute during parsing even though the injected tags are async.
findings.location: All public pages head lines ~5-7; contrast quote.html:4-7
findings.recommendation: Port quote.html's idle-deferred GTM/Clarity bootstrap to the shared head template of the other 25 pages.

findings.title: Script defer usage inconsistent: blog posts defer main.js/chat-widget.js, the 15 root marketing pages do not
findings.evidence: All 7 blog posts: <script src="../js/main.js?v=4.6" defer> + <script src="../js/chat-widget.js?v=4.3" defer> (e.g. blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html:159-160). All 15 root marketing pages + quote-janitorial + quote-dayporter load the same files with NO defer (e.g. index.html:547-548, at body end so impact is parser-blocking only at the tail). quote.html: main.js no defer (line 1186), quote-flow.js defer (line 1194). HubSpot is async defer where present; Turnstile on legacy quote pages is async defer.
findings.location: index.html:547-548 and equivalent body-end blocks on about/services/janitorial/day-porter/why-ecco/testimonials/careers/sustainability/blog/accessibility/privacy/terms/sitemap/404/quote-janitorial/quote-dayporter; blog/*.html:~159-160
findings.recommendation: Standardize on defer for main.js and chat-widget.js everywhere (matches blog pattern, lets the preload scanner fetch earlier from head instead of being discovered at body end).

findings.title: Render-blocking Google Fonts CSS on 25 pages; only quote.html uses the async media=print pattern
findings.evidence: Marketing pages: plain render-blocking <link rel=stylesheet> to fonts.googleapis.com (DM Sans 4 weights + Cormorant Garamond 4 weights = 8 styles, display=swap, preconnect to both font hosts present on all checked pages). quote.html: same-host CSS loaded with media="print" onload="this.media='all'" + <noscript> mirror (lines 56-57), 13 styles across 3 families (DM Sans 7 incl. 3 italics, Fraunces 5, Caveat 1). NOTE: the audit premise of a 'duplicate font <link>' on quote.html is FALSE — the second identical link is inside <noscript> (line 57), standard fallback, no double fetch.
findings.recommendation: Apply quote.html's media=print+onload+noscript font pattern to the marketing template; audit whether all 4 Cormorant weights and DM Sans 700 italic are actually used before re-requesting.

findings.title: fetchpriority="high" spent on the 180x40 nav logo instead of the LCP hero on every page
findings.evidence: The only fetchpriority on each marketing page is (e.g. index.html:132, services.html:39, blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html:41). The real LCP candidate is the .hero-img inline-style CSS background (index.html:150 url('images/stock/4.webp') 216,088B), which cannot carry fetchpriority; mitigated by on 14 of 15 hero pages — but accessibility.html has a hero-img (hero-office.webp) and NO preload (preload_img=0, hero-img=1).
findings.location: Nav logo img on all 25 pages; preload lines ~29-30 of each page head; accessibility.html (missing preload)
findings.recommendation: Drop fetchpriority=high from the nav logo (or set low), add the missing preload to accessibility.html, and long-term convert heroes to elements.

findings.title: ~10.4MB of deployed images are referenced by nothing (incl. 5.2MB of two PNG screenshots)
findings.evidence: 28 of 56 files in images/stock (~9.55MB) have zero references in any HTML/CSS/JS even after URL-encoding-aware search: worst offenders 681792a66d3b3-1746375334.png 3,156,732B and 681792a45dd84-1746375332.png 2,018,051B, Cleaningdisinfectingandsanitizingshare.jpg 772,107B, Allergy_share.jpg 718,951B, 8-nyc-skyline-night.jpg 392,770B, hero-office.jpg 254,344B, plus 17 orphaned .jpg originals whose .webp siblings are the referenced variant, plus exact duplicate '61392d63add9bc08084ffa07_what-is-janitorial-services.jpeg' and ' (1).jpeg' (319,684B each). Also unreferenced: the entire images/spaces dir (8 files, ~260KB, added Apr 25), images/alina-avatar.jpg 584,849B (responsive 96/192/480 variants are what's used), images/logo-footer.png 55,654B, 'images/Ecco Facilities LLC Logo  (Logotipos) (8).png' 48,176B. All are git-tracked and served with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable.
findings.location: images/stock/ (56 files, 14.4MB), images/spaces/ (unreferenced in *.html, blog/*.html, crm/*.html, css/*.css, js/*.js), images/alina-avatar.jpg, images/logo-footer.png
findings.recommendation: Delete or move to a non-deployed archive after a final encoded-name grep across all HTML (per project memory on cross-page CSS/asset purges); cuts repo and deploy size from ~15MB to ~4.5MB.

findings.title: Dev artifacts tracked in git and publicly deployed: index-reference.html, mobile-test.html, emailjs-template.html, *.original.js
findings.evidence: git ls-files confirms tracked: index-reference.html (29,925B old homepage with own font URL), mobile-test.html (2,068B), emailjs-template.html (3,545B), js/chat-widget.original.js (22,116B), js/main.original.js (4,950B) — none referenced by any page, all fetchable on eccofacilities.com and crawlable (robots.txt only disallows /admin/). COUNTER-EVIDENCE to the audit premise: quote.backup.html, js/quote-flow.backup.js (101,318B), css/quote-flow.source.css (339,733B), mockups.html + mockups/ (1.7MB), font-compare.html, font-specimen.html, color-swatches.html, css/hero-*.source.css are gitignored (*.backup.*, mockups/, css/*.source.css rules) and NOT in git ls-files => NOT deployed; they are local-working-tree clutter only.
findings.location: /index-reference.html, /mobile-test.html, /emailjs-template.html, /js/chat-widget.original.js, /js/main.original.js (deployed); .gitignore lines for *.backup.*, mockups/, css/*.source.css (proof of non-deployment of the rest)
findings.recommendation: Untrack and delete the five deployed dev files (index-reference.html duplicates homepage content — SEO duplicate risk too); keep local-only artifacts out of the deploy as already configured.

findings.title: HubSpot tracking missing on the two legacy quote pages only
findings.evidence: hs-scripts.com/245755967.js present on 24 pages (async defer) but hubspot=0 on quote-janitorial.html and quote-dayporter.html (clarity=1 gtm=2 on both). These are live conversion pages still linked from the site.
findings.location: quote-janitorial.html, quote-dayporter.html vs every other public page
findings.recommendation: Either add the HubSpot loader for parity or confirm the omission is intentional (e.g. pages slated for removal) and document it.

findings.title: quote-flow.css shows append-accretion: 444 duplicate selector re-declarations in a 210KB single-page stylesheet
findings.evidence: css/quote-flow.css (210,488B minified, one page) contains 1,590 rules / 1,146 unique selectors; repeated re-declarations: .qf-screen x9, .qf-flow-bar x8, .qf-service-card x7, .qf-rev-foot x7, .qf-rev-btn x7, 'main.q-flow .qf-rev-edit' x7 (some legit media-query splits, but the magnitude tracks the D1-D129 patch history and ?v=50.3 churn). styles.css similarly: 398 dup re-declarations (.day-btn x6, .ind-grid x5, .stage-hero-avatar x5). quote-flow.css also embeds 12 data:image URIs vs 2 in styles.css.
findings.location: css/quote-flow.css (minified; readable source css/quote-flow.source.css 339,733B local-only); css/styles.css
findings.recommendation: Consolidate per-selector declarations in the source CSS and re-minify (bunx clean-css-cli per project rule, watching the known @layer/:root stripping bug); target meaningful reduction of the 35.5KB gzip payload.

findings.title: Deployed cache-buster versions are internally consistent; only the untracked local backup drifts
findings.evidence: Census across tracked pages: styles.css?v=15.1 x25, main.js?v=4.6 x25, chat-widget.js?v=4.3 x24, cookie-consent.js?v=1.1 x25, quote-flow.css?v=50.3 x1, quote-flow.js?v=38.0 x1 — no cross-page drift on deployed files. The only outliers (styles.css?v=13.7, quote-flow.css?v=9.0, quote-flow.js?v=9.0, main.js?v=4.4) live exclusively in quote.backup.html, which is gitignored/untracked and not deployed. The 'v=15.1 vs v=50.3' premise compares two different files, not a drift.
findings.recommendation: No action needed beyond deleting quote.backup.html locally; keep the bump-in-same-commit rule.

findings.title: Blog post body image lacks width/height attributes (inline-style sizing instead) on all 7 posts
findings.evidence: Each blog post has 4 ; 3 carry width/height, the body image does not: (blog/janitorial-vs-day-porter.html and same pattern in the other 6 posts). CLS risk is mostly mitigated by the fixed inline height, but it violates the zero-inline-styles rule and skips intrinsic-size hints.
findings.recommendation: Add width/height attributes and move sizing to a CSS class.

findings.title: quote.html footer requests Cormorant Garamond that the page never loads (silent serif fallback)
findings.evidence: styles.css rule .footer-cta-text{font-family:var(--fd);font-size:1.35rem;...} where --fd='Cormorant Garamond'; quote.html's only Google Fonts URL (line 56) contains DM Sans + Fraunces + Caveat — zero Cormorant. The shared footer on quote.html therefore renders .footer-cta-text in the generic serif fallback, visibly different from the same footer on the other 24 pages. (Perf upside: avoids a 4th font family; the inconsistency is the issue.)
findings.location: quote.html:56 (font URL) + shared footer markup; css/styles.css .footer-cta-text
findings.recommendation: Either override the footer serif with Fraunces in quote-flow.css scope or accept and document the fallback; do not add Cormorant to quote.html just for one element.

findings.title: chart.min.js and sortable.min.js correctly scoped to CRM but parked in the public /js directory
findings.evidence: grep -l across all HTML: chart.min.js only in crm/index.html, sortable.min.js only in crm/pipeline.html — no public page loads them (premise verified clean). Both are git-tracked and publicly fetchable at /js/chart.min.js (205,889B) and /js/sortable.min.js (45,092B); /crm/* has X-Robots-Tag noindex + no-cache headers but /js/* gets public max-age=2592000.
findings.recommendation: Optional: move CRM-only vendor libs under /crm/ so the CRM CSP and robots/noindex surface covers them; no public page-load impact today.

findings.title: Nav/footer logos are PNG (30-70KB) repeated on every page; no SVG/WebP in use
findings.evidence: images/logo-horizontal.png 30,809B (light nav, 25+ pages incl. CRM login) and images/logo-horizontal-white.png 69,568B (footer/dark nav, 24 pages). An SVG logo exists in the repo ('images/stock/Ecco Facilities LLC Logo  (Logotipos) (2).svg', 65,844B, unreferenced) and a webp variant (24,192B, unreferenced). Cached after first page so impact is first-visit only.
findings.location: images/logo-horizontal.png, images/logo-horizontal-white.png; all page nav/footer blocks
findings.recommendation: Export a clean optimized SVG (a hand-optimized one should be a few KB, not 65KB) or WebP for both logo variants.

reports.inventory:
WEIGHT TABLE (raw/gzip bytes, deployed=git-tracked):
CSS: styles.css 169,918/30,807 (min, all 25 pages, v=15.1) | quote-flow.css 210,488/35,532 (min, quote.html only, v=50.3) | crm.css 36,469 (CRM only). Local-only untracked: quote-flow.source.css 339,733; hero-*.source.css x4 ~17,336.
JS: quote-flow.js 264,947/73,552 UNMINIFIED 955 comment lines (quote.html, v=38.0) | chart.min.js 205,889/69,897 (crm/index.html only) | chat-widget.js 56,016 unmin (24 pages, v=4.3) | sortable.min.js 45,092 (crm/pipeline.html only) | main.js 21,083 unmin (25 pages, v=4.6) | chat-widget.original.js 22,116 DEPLOYED-ORPHAN | main.original.js 4,950 DEPLOYED-ORPHAN | cookie-consent.js 1,776 (v=1.1). Local-only: quote-flow.backup.js 101,318.
IMAGES: 15MB total, 81 tracked files (36 jpg, 28 webp, 9 png, 4 avif, 1 svg). images/stock 56 files 14.4MB — 28 unreferenced (~9.55MB) incl. 2 PNGs 3,156,732+2,018,051. images/spaces 8 files 260KB — 100% unreferenced. >300KB files (12): 3.16MB png, 2.02MB png, 853KB 'Safe for sensitivities.jpg'*, 772KB Cleaningdisinfecting*, 719KB Allergy_share, 585KB alina-avatar.jpg(orphan), 565KB 'Residential Buildings.jpg'*, 523KB 'Safe for children.jpg'*, 393KB 8-nyc-skyline-night.jpg(orphan), 392KB 'Corporate Offices.webp'(blog), 2x 319,684 what-is-janitorial dupes(orphan). *=live on index.html as inline-style CSS backgrounds.
PAGE PAYLOADS (first-party gzip): index.html ~65KB JS+CSS+HTML but references 4,001,233B images (2.62MB eager JPEG backgrounds). quote.html ~167KB gz / 760,145B raw (html 91,933 + styles 169,918 + quote-flow.css 210,488 + quote-flow.js 264,947 + main 21,083 + cookie 1,776) + 13 font styles 3 families; styles.css =31% potentially used there.
DEAD CSS: styles.css 661 classes, 203 zero-ref (197 excl. dynamic anim-*), 402/1706 rules fully dead = 43,433B = 25.6%. Dup selector re-decls: styles.css 398, quote-flow.css 444.
ANALYTICS MATRIX: clarity x26 pages (parse-time head except quote.html idle-deferred), GTM x26 (same split), HubSpot x24 (missing: quote-janitorial, quote-dayporter). Turnstile async defer on 2 legacy quote pages.
FONTS: marketing 2 families/8 styles render-blocking + preconnect x2; quote.html 3 families/13 styles async (media=print+onload+noscript, NOT a duplicate link). CACHE: _headers — images 1yr immutable, css/js 30d, html 1h. VERIFIED-NOT-DEPLOYED (gitignored): quote.backup.html, mockups.html+mockups/ 1.7MB, font-compare/font-specimen/color-swatches.html, css/*.source.css, *.backup.js.

reports.name: js-conducta
reports.summary: Public JS is split across 4 live files (main.js 438L, chat-widget.js 901L, cookie-consent.js 33L, quote-flow.js 5435L) plus 128KB of dead .original/.backup JS still deployed. quote-flow.js is in surprisingly good health (0 real TODOs, hardened XSS/consent/draft logic, robust submit failure handling) but carries documented V1 dead branches and one P1 (exit-intent capture shows success toast on failed POST; chat-handoff prefill params written but never read). The worst systemic defect is consent: privacy.html §4 claims zero tracking while GTM + Clarity load unconditionally on all 26 public pages (including privacy.html itself) and HubSpot loads ungated on 22 of 24 pages — cookie-consent.js gates nothing except a single-pageview HubSpot doNotTrack push, and the GPC path is a no-op because window._hsq doesn't exist when it runs. main.js contains two competing .svc-tab implementations that actively break services.html (both panels become .active after any tab click) plus 4 fully dead feature blocks (.faq-q, .ind-card, [data-parallax], .rv-light/.rv-child — zero HTML consumers). chat-widget.js is hard-broken on all 7 blog pages (relative avatar/quote.html URLs 404 from /blog/) and embodies a third de-facto design system (130+ JS-injected CSS rules with off-token colors, em dashes, Title Case, urgency copy contradicting the qf2 Alina brand rules). localStorage uses three naming conventions across 8 keys. No public page loads crm-*.js (confirmed: only crm/*.html reference them).

findings.title: privacy.html claims no tracking while loading GTM + Clarity + HubSpot unconditionally on every page including privacy.html itself
findings.evidence: privacy.html:127-128 states 'We do not deploy third-party tracking cookies, analytics pixels, or retargeting technologies... does not use cookies for advertising or behavioral tracking purposes.' Yet privacy.html:5 loads GTM-W2ZWXZ3T, privacy.html:7 loads Microsoft Clarity tag w546w8zoh2 (session-recording/behavioral analytics), and privacy.html:230-232 loads HubSpot 245755967 — all before/without any consent. GTM+Clarity inline snippets appear on 26 pages (19 root incl. 404/terms/privacy/quote.backup + 7 blog); HubSpot <script src="//js-na2.hs-scripts.com/245755967.js"> loads unconditionally on 22 pages. cookie-consent.js gates none of them.
findings.location: privacy.html:5,7,127-128,230-232; same GTM/Clarity head snippets on 404.html, about.html, accessibility.html, blog.html, careers.html, day-porter.html, index.html (lines 4-7), janitorial.html, quote-janitorial.html, quote-dayporter.html, sitemap.html, services.html, why-ecco.html, testimonials.html, sustainability.html, quote.html, terms.html, quote.backup.html, blog/*.html (7 files)
findings.recommendation: Either rewrite privacy.html §4 to disclose GTM/GA4, Clarity, and HubSpot truthfully, or implement real consent gating (load all three trackers only after accept, Google Consent Mode default-denied). Current state is a legal-factual contradiction on a CCPA-era policy page.

findings.title: Consent system is cosmetic: decline never propagates to GTM/Clarity, HubSpot doNotTrack fires only on the decline pageview, GPC path is a silent no-op
findings.evidence: cookie-consent.js:9 'if(localStorage.getItem(`ecco_cookies`))return;' — on every pageview after a decline, the script exits before any doNotTrack push, so declined users are fully tracked by HubSpot/GTM/Clarity on all subsequent pages. Decline handler (lines 25-32) only pushes ['doNotTrack'] to window._hsq and does nothing for GTM/Clarity. GPC path (lines 3-8) runs synchronously at parse time, before the async hs-script-loader executes, and uses 'if(window._hsq)window._hsq.push(...)' without creating the queue ('window._hsq = window._hsq || []'), so the signal is lost — GPC users are tracked normally. GPC path also sets only ecco_cookies, never ecco_consent, so quote-flow.js hasConsent() (quote-flow.js:532-543) and the quote.html HubSpot gate see 'no decision' instead of declined. Only quote.html:1196-1215 and quote.backup.html gate HubSpot on consent; the other 22 pages load it via plain script tag.
findings.location: js/cookie-consent.js:3-9,25-32; quote.html:1196-1215; index.html:559 (ungated hs-script-loader, pattern repeated on 22 pages)
findings.recommendation: Create the _hsq queue before pushing; persist doNotTrack on every pageview for declined/GPC users; set both consent keys in the GPC path; extend the quote.html-style gated HubSpot loader to all pages; add consent gating for GTM/Clarity (they are currently never gated anywhere).

findings.title: Chat widget is broken on all 7 blog pages: avatar 404s and every chat link (including the primary quote CTA) points to /blog/* dead paths
findings.evidence: chat-widget.js builds all URLs relative: CONFIG.avatar='images/alina-avatar-192.jpg' (line 78, rendered in toggle button + panel header at line 216), CTA bar href='quote.html' (line 216), buildQuoteUrl returns 'quote.html?...' (line 722), and all 10 fallback responses link quote.html/sustainability.html/day-porter.html/janitorial.html/careers.html (lines 403-415). All 7 blog/*.html load the widget via src="../js/chat-widget.js?v=4.3"; URLs resolve against /blog/ — blog/ contains only 7 HTML files (no images/, no quote.html), and zero blog pages have a <base> tag (grep -c '<base' = 0 for all 7). Result: broken avatar image on the always-visible toggle, and the 'Start your free quote · 24-hour turnaround' CTA plus the entire chat mini-quote funnel dead-end in 404s on blog pages.
findings.recommendation: Root-relative all widget URLs ('/images/alina-avatar-192.jpg', '/quote.html') or derive a base path from the script's own src.

findings.title: main.js ships two competing .svc-tab implementations; the second corrupts services.html (both panels become active) and desyncs aria-hidden on index mobile
findings.evidence: Handler A (main.js:81-94) keys on tab.dataset.panel and toggles panels by p.id; handler B initSvcTabs (main.js:203-230) keys on getAttribute('data-svc'). services.html:84-85 tabs use data-panel only and services.html:90,134 panels have id but NO data-svc; index.html:201-211 uses data-svc only. On services.html any tab click runs B with target=null, and 'p.getAttribute(`data-svc`) === target' is null===null → true for BOTH panels → classList.add('active') on both. CSS '.svc-panel.active{opacity:1;...;position:relative}' (css/styles.css) renders both panels stacked. Reverse bug on index ≤900px: handler A strips .active from all panels (p.id===undefined) then calls syncSvcAria() (main.js:66-77) which sets aria-hidden="true" on every panel; handler B then re-adds .active without re-syncing, leaving the visible panel aria-hidden="true".
findings.recommendation: Delete one implementation and converge both pages on a single data attribute; re-run syncSvcAria after the final active state is set.

findings.title: Chat-to-quote handoff silently discards 4 of 7 collected answers: STATE.prefill* is written but never read
findings.evidence: chat-widget.js mini-wizard collects spaceType/sqft/frequency/urgency/firstName/email/phone (QUOTE_STEPS lines 590-598) and passes all 7 as URL params (buildQuoteUrl lines 713-723). quote-flow.js prefillFromUrl (4935-4965) fills the 4 contact inputs, then stashes space/size/freq/urgency as STATE.prefillSpace/prefillSize/prefillFreq/prefillUrgency (4959-4962) with comment 'for the flow to pick up later' — grep shows exactly 4 occurrences of prefill* in the file, all writes, zero reads. Users who answer the chat's 4 qualifying questions and click 'Send my quote' must re-answer all 4 in the wizard.
findings.recommendation: Wire STATE.prefill* into the space/size/schedule screens (preselect cards) or stop collecting those 4 answers in the chat funnel.

findings.title: Exit-intent email capture shows success ('We've got your spot... we'll follow up') even when the POST failed — silent lead loss plus a false promise
findings.evidence: quote-flow.js:4850-4862 — fetch('/api/capture-partial').catch(function(){ /* silent — backend may be offline */ }).finally(function(){ ... qfToast({type:'success', title:'We’ve got your spot', message:'We’ll follow up with ' + val + '...'}) ... }). The success toast and qfExitClose run unconditionally in .finally, so a network failure or 5xx loses the lead while telling the user Ecco will contact them. Contrast: the main submit path (4516-4554) correctly differentiates 429/403/5xx/network and never fakes success.
findings.recommendation: Branch on response.ok: on failure show the error toast with the info@eccofacilities.com fallback (pattern already exists at 4528-4543) and keep the modal open.

findings.title: Three parallel quote funnels with three different email validators and duplicated wizard logic
findings.evidence: Funnel 1: quote-flow.js (quote.html) with strict EMAIL_RE /^(?!\.)(?!.*\.\.)[A-Za-z0-9._%+\-]+(?<!\.)@.../ (1470) 'MUST match functions/api/submit-quote.js byte-for-byte' (1466). Funnel 2: chat-widget.js QUOTE_STEPS (590-598) with loose /^[^@\s]+@[^@\s]+\.[^@\s]+$/ (596) — accepts emails the real form/backend rejects, then hands off via URL. Funnel 3: inline wizards in quote-janitorial.html (fetch /api/submit-quote line 577, own validateEmail via onblur line 241) and quote-dayporter.html (line 682), which also fire an empty navigator.sendBeacon('https://www.google-analytics.com/collect','') on abandon (quote-janitorial.html:626). Same endpoint, three divergent client validations and three copies of step logic.
findings.recommendation: Make the chat mini-wizard reuse EMAIL_RE-equivalent validation (or validate server-side on handoff); plan retirement of the legacy inline wizards; drop the empty GA beacon.

findings.title: Production console statements: 2 in quote-flow.js (1 production-reachable), 5 in inline page scripts, plus a global console.warn monkey-patch on quote.html
findings.evidence: js/quote-flow.js:182 console.warn('[qf] Missing review/edit elements:'...) — localhost-gated (host check at 175, NOT a production violation); js/quote-flow.js:1778 console.warn('[quote] Google Places failed to load...') — production-reachable. Inline: quote.html:148 console.warn('[quote] Places init failed...'); quote.html:76-82 globally replaces console.warn to filter Google Maps deprecation spam (monkey-patches a built-in for all scripts on the page); quote-janitorial.html:583 and quote-dayporter.html:688 console.error('Submit error:'...+JSON.stringify(data)) — logs backend error payloads; quote.backup.html:68,74 two console.warn. main.js, chat-widget.js, cookie-consent.js: 0 each.
findings.recommendation: Per project rule 'No console.log in production code': strip or gate quote-flow.js:1778 and quote.html:148 behind the existing localhost check; replace console.error in legacy wizards with the toast pattern; reconsider the console.warn override (scope filtering to the Maps callback instead).

findings.title: main.js carries 4 fully dead feature blocks plus orphan reveal-system branches (rv-light/rv-child have zero HTML consumers)
findings.evidence: Selector census across all 25 public pages: .faq-q/.faq-item → 0 pages (FAQ accordion, main.js:3 mid-IIFE block, ~10 lines dead); .ind-card → 0 pages (initIndustryAccordion 187-198 dead); [data-parallax] → 0 pages (initParallax 235-273 dead, exits at L240 before registering its scroll listener); .rv-light → 0 pages (observer registration main.js:127 dead) and .rv-child → 0 pages (stagger logic in the main IntersectionObserver callback and 3s fallback, main.js:3, dead) — note css/styles.css still contains 2 rv-light and 2 rv-child rule occurrences for these unused classes; unused variable prefersReducedMotion declared main.js:3, 0 reads (noMotion is the one used). Project-rule check 'no rv-light/rv-child on above-the-fold content': zero misuse because zero usage anywhere — the entire rv-light/rv-child subsystem is vestigial. Also chat-widget.js:540 'function maybeShowScrollFab() { /* deprecated */ }' empty stub. All anim-* hero-rotate classes verified present in styles.css (anim-team x17 etc.) — no purge damage there.
findings.recommendation: Delete the four dead blocks and the stub; decide whether rv-light/rv-child is being revived or remove its JS+CSS together (remember the cross-page grep lesson before purging CSS).

findings.title: localStorage namespace uses 3 naming conventions, dual consent keys, a stale abandoned theme key, and a consent cookie check nothing ever sets
findings.evidence: Census (all 4 files): snake_case ecco_cookies + ecco_consent (cookie-consent.js:5,19-20,26-27), ecco_quote_draft_v1 (quote-flow.js:528), ecco_quote_last_submit_ts (quote-flow.js:4405); camelCase eccoChatState_v1 (chat-widget.js:780) + eccoNudgeDismissed_<pageKey> (chat-widget.js:559,584); bare prefix qf_exit_shown in sessionStorage (quote-flow.js:4791). ecco_cookies and ecco_consent store the identical accepted/declined state in two keys ('legacy key used by HubSpot gating' per cookie-consent.js:15-17). ecco_theme is no longer read or written — left stale on devices per D128 comment (quote-flow.js:4766-4770). hasConsent() (quote-flow.js:540-541) and the quote.html HubSpot gate (quote.html:1208) also check a document.cookie 'ecco_consent=' value that no code ever sets — dead branch.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on one ecco_ snake_case prefix, collapse the dual consent keys behind one accessor, delete the dead cookie checks, and one-time-purge ecco_theme.

findings.title: Chat widget persists conversation history (free-text PII) to localStorage for 24h with no consent check, while quote-flow strips PII without consent — contradictory privacy posture between the two Alina surfaces
findings.evidence: chat-widget.js saveState() (783-791) writes the full conversationHistory to eccoChatState_v1 after every exchange with no hasConsent() equivalent; users typing emails/phones in chat have them persisted unconditionally (TTL 24h, line 781). quote-flow.js explicitly strips PII_FIELDS from drafts unless ecco_consent==='accepted' (525-562) and purges the draft on ecco:consent-declined (603-606). Same site, same persona, opposite rules.
findings.recommendation: Apply the quote-flow consent gate to chat persistence (skip saveState or store nothing identifying without consent; listen for ecco:consent-declined to clearState).

findings.title: 128KB of dead JS deployed publicly plus stale cache busters on quote.backup.html
findings.evidence: js/chat-widget.original.js (22,116 B), js/main.original.js (4,950 B), js/quote-flow.backup.js (101,318 B) are referenced by zero HTML files (grep across *.html, blog/*.html, crm/*.html: no hits). quote.backup.html (publicly deployed dev artifact) loads js/quote-flow.js?v=9.0 (quote.backup.html:1038) and js/main.js?v=4.4 — vs v=38.0 and v=4.6 on quote.html — so the current 265KB quote-flow.js executes against ancient markup there (silently no-ops; the missing-ID assertion at quote-flow.js:172-186 is localhost-only). Cache-buster rule says busters bump with the file; the backup page was left behind.
findings.location: js/chat-widget.original.js; js/main.original.js; js/quote-flow.backup.js; quote.backup.html:1038 and its main.js tag
findings.recommendation: Delete the three dead JS files and quote.backup.html from the deploy (or move to .dead-archive/), per the existing archive convention.

findings.title: quote-flow.js V1 leftovers: dead legacy screen handlers and ~94 references to retired qfSum/qfRv/qfEdit IDs as no-op calls, all awaiting 'Phase 3'
findings.evidence: quote-flow.js:129 'D55 Phase 3 — legacy dpDays/porter/hours screens removed'; 161 'Legacy V1 handler vars below short-circuit harmlessly'; 264-266 'still get written by the legacy dpDays / porter / hours handlers... Once Phase 3 lands they can be removed'; live dead branches at 1183 (if name==='dpDays') and 1207 (if name==='hours') for screens that no longer exist; 176-178 'V1→V2 redesign retired the qfSum*/qfRv*/qfEdit* IDs; setVal() calls on those are dead-code no-ops'; grep count of qfSum|qfRv|qfEdit = 94 occurrences; 352-353 and 957-959 document D147/D149 V1 flow-bar/rail elements that 'no longer exist' with guards still in place. TODO/FIXME/HACK comments: 0 real (2 grep hits are XXX placeholders in phone/ZIP comments).
findings.recommendation: Execute the documented Phase 3 cleanup: delete dpDays/hours branches, legacy STATE fields, and the 94 no-op setVal references. The file is otherwise clean (no TODOs, consistent failure handling, draft preserved on submit failure so no data loss).

findings.title: Chat widget is a third de-facto design system: 130+ JS-injected CSS rules with off-token colors and copy that violates the brand rules governing the same Alina persona
findings.evidence: chat-widget.js:84-212 injects a full stylesheet via JS with hardcoded hex: chat background #F5F3EE (lines 160,171 — matches neither System A --cream #F3F5F8 nor qf2 --qf2-cream #EEF2ED; looks like transposed digits of System A's cream), badge red #E54848 (line 90, vs --red #C84444), grays #94A3B5/#5B6A84/#DFE4EC/#EDF0F5/#E5EBF2 from neither token set, plus 6 gradient backgrounds (lines 120,145-146,167,178) where qf2 brand direction bans gradient accents. Copy: 3 of 10 page contexts use Title Case persona titles ('Cleaning Experience Advisor', lines 42,47,52) vs sentence case elsewhere ('Your janitorial guide'); greetings use em dashes throughout (lines 8,13,23,28,33...) and urgency copy 'Start your free quote · 24-hour turnaround' (line 216) and fake-availability 'Online · Replies in ~2 min' (line 216) — all contradict the PRODUCT.md rules (sentence case, no em dashes, no urgency) for the same Alina who fronts /quote. Footer disclosure '🤖 AI-powered assistant · Not a real person' (line 81) is good and should be kept.
findings.recommendation: Move widget CSS to a real stylesheet using System A tokens; normalize all persona titles to sentence case; align greeting copy with the qf2 voice rules since both surfaces are 'Alina'.

findings.title: GTM/Clarity loading strategy inconsistent: deferred via load+requestIdleCallback on quote.html only, immediate parse-time on the other 25 pages
findings.evidence: quote.html:5,7 wrap both snippets in window.addEventListener('load', ...) + requestIdleCallback(f,{timeout:2500}) fallback setTimeout 1500/1800ms; index.html:4-7, privacy.html:5,7 and the remaining 23 pages run the identical IIFEs synchronously in <head>. Same trackers, two performance profiles; whichever pattern is intended, 25 pages drift from it.
findings.location: quote.html:5,7 vs index.html:4-7, privacy.html:5,7 and 23 more pages
findings.recommendation: Pick one loader (the quote.html deferred variant is the better one) and template it across all pages — ideally combined with consent gating from the P0/P1 findings.

findings.title: Cookie banner violates the zero-inline-styles rule and uses global onclick handlers
findings.evidence: cookie-consent.js:12 builds the banner with innerHTML containing '<div style="display:flex;gap:.5rem">' (inline style attribute) and onclick="acceptCookies()" / onclick="declineCookies()" requiring window-global functions (defined lines 18,25). Project rule: 'Zero inline styles — all styling through CSS classes'. Also only works because CSP allows 'unsafe-inline'.
findings.recommendation: Replace the inline style with a .cookie-actions class and wire buttons with addEventListener; drop the window globals.

findings.title: Chat widget globally hijacks Cmd/Ctrl+K on 24 pages and duplicates the open-toggle logic in two places
findings.evidence: chat-widget.js:896-897 document-level keydown intercepts (metaKey||ctrlKey)+'k' with preventDefault on every page that loads the widget (24 pages) — conflicting with browser/extension search shortcuts; the handler body is a verbatim copy of the toggle click handler (881-890), a second copy of open/restore/greet logic that has already drifted risk. Focus trap at 899 hardcodes f=[closeBtn,resetBtn,input,sendBtn]: resetBtn actually precedes closeBtn in DOM (line 216) so 'first' is wrong, and the CTA bar link/quick-reply buttons are excluded from the wrap, letting Shift+Tab from resetBtn escape the aria-modal dialog.
findings.recommendation: Remove or gate the global shortcut; extract a single openChat() used by both handlers; compute focusables dynamically inside the panel.

findings.title: EMAIL_RE lookbehind breaks quote-flow.js parsing on Safari <16.4 / Chrome <62, contradicting the file's own legacy-browser posture
findings.evidence: quote-flow.js:1470 regex literal contains (?<!\.) lookbehind; engines without lookbehind support throw SyntaxError at parse time, killing the entire 265KB IIFE (form completely dead, no fallback). The same file deliberately supports ancient browsers elsewhere: line 650 'Legacy string (Chrome <51, Edge)' beforeunload copy, var-only style, 'IntersectionObserver' in window guards.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite EMAIL_RE without lookbehind (e.g. explicit char-class ending) or accept and document a Safari 16.4+/Chrome 62+ floor and delete the Chrome<51 accommodations.

findings.title: Hero rotate interval runs forever and hero stats render as '0' without JavaScript
findings.evidence: main.js:380-383 setInterval every 3400ms rebuilds .word-layer DOM indefinitely with no document.hidden pause and no teardown (reduced-motion is respected at 378). index.html:162-165 hero stats markup is 0 — noscript users see '0 Years Serving NYC', '0 Businesses Trust Us', '0 Boroughs Covered' (factually wrong copy); the 3s fallback (main.js:172-181) still requires JS. Also three separate count-up implementations coexist: animateCounter for .stat-num (main.js:3, used by about/testimonials .stats-bar), initCounters for .hero-stat-num (134-182, index only), and the success-ref count-up in quote-flow.js:4625-4636 — same ease-out cubic logic written three times.
findings.recommendation: Pause the rotate interval on visibilitychange; put real numbers in the HTML and animate from 0 only when JS runs; long-term consolidate one counter helper.

findings.title: quote.backup.html legacy Places loader uses inline onerror with console.warn; legacy pages rely on inline event handlers throughout
findings.evidence: quote.backup.html:74 <script ... onerror="console.warn('[quote] Google Places autocomplete failed to load...')"> — inline handler + console in a publicly deployed dev artifact. quote-janitorial.html:241 uses oninput=/onblur= inline handlers for validation (pattern repeated across both legacy wizards), working only because CSP retains 'unsafe-inline'.
findings.recommendation: Covered by deleting quote.backup.html (see dead-files finding); when legacy wizards are retired, the inline-handler debt goes with them.

reports.inventory:
CONSOLE CENSUS (production rule: none) — js/main.js: 0; js/chat-widget.js: 0; js/cookie-consent.js: 0; js/quote-flow.js: 2 console.warn (L182 localhost-gated, L1778 production-reachable). Inline page scripts: quote.html L148 warn + L76-82 global console.warn monkey-patch; quote-janitorial.html L583 error; quote-dayporter.html L688 error; quote.backup.html L68+L74 warn.
SCRIPT LOAD CENSUS — js/main.js?v=4.6: 25 pages (+v=4.4 on quote.backup.html); js/chat-widget.js?v=4.3: 24 pages (17 root + 7 blog; NOT on quote.html — D125); js/cookie-consent.js?v=1.1: 26 pages; js/quote-flow.js: quote.html (v=38.0) + quote.backup.html (v=9.0). GTM-W2ZWXZ3T + Clarity w546w8zoh2 inline: 26 pages, unconditional (deferred only on quote.html). HubSpot 245755967: 22 pages unconditional + 2 consent-gated (quote.html, quote.backup.html). crm-*.js: loaded ONLY by crm/*.html — zero public-page references. Dead deployed JS: chat-widget.original.js (22KB), main.original.js (5KB), quote-flow.backup.js (101KB) — zero references.
STORAGE KEY CENSUS — localStorage: ecco_cookies (cookie-consent w/r, quote-flow r), ecco_consent (cookie-consent w, quote-flow r, quote.html gate r), ecco_quote_draft_v1 (quote-flow, PII consent-gated, 7d TTL), ecco_quote_last_submit_ts (quote-flow, 60s cooldown), eccoChatState_v1 (chat-widget, 24h TTL, NOT consent-gated), eccoNudgeDismissed_<pageKey> (chat-widget), ecco_theme (stale, orphaned since D128). sessionStorage: qf_exit_shown (quote-flow). Cookie 'ecco_consent' read in 2 places, never written. 3 naming conventions: ecco_snake, eccoCamel, qf_.
ENDPOINTS — quote-flow.js: POST /api/submit-quote (Turnstile-tokened, differentiated 429/403/5xx/network errors, draft preserved on failure = no data loss), POST /api/capture-partial (errors swallowed, false-success toast); chat-widget.js: POST https://ecco-chat-backend.vercel.app/api/chat (15s AbortController timeout → canned fallback + retry banner; matches CSP connect-src); legacy quote-janitorial/dayporter inline: same two /api endpoints + empty sendBeacon to google-analytics.com/collect on abandon.
DUPLICATE LOGIC MAP — 2 .svc-tab tab systems (main.js L81-94 vs L203-230, active conflict); 3 quote funnels (quote-flow.js wizard / chat-widget QUOTE_STEPS / legacy inline wizards) with 3 different email validators; 3 count-up animators (main.js x2, quote-flow x1); 2 smooth-scroll approaches (main.js native scrollTo vs quote-flow custom easeInOutCubic smoothScrollTo L4872); 2 copies of chat open-logic (click + ⌘K handlers); 2 focus-trap implementations (chat-widget L899 hardcoded list vs quote-flow L4824 query-based). Theme systems: only ONE remains (qf2 CSS prefers-color-scheme auto per D128; JS toggle fully removed, no [data-theme] JS writes anywhere — verified).
RV REVEAL SYSTEM — .rv used on 12 pages (about 8 … janitorial 36), zero above-the-fold misuse found (0 occurrences in first 200 lines of all main pages, heroes clean); .rv-light/.rv-child: 0 consumers anywhere (JS + 2 CSS rules each = dead subsystem). anim-health/team/people/planet/future/budget dynamic classes all present in styles.css (no purge damage).

reports.name: a11y-sistemica
reports.summary: Systemic accessibility is two-tier: the quote V2 flow (quote.html + quote-flow.js) has mature patterns (inert on done screens, role=alert toasts, aria-invalid/aria-describedby management, 39 prefers-reduced-motion blocks, 16px mobile inputs), while the marketing shell carries cross-page failures on every page: AA contrast failures in both token systems (--tm 4.23:1 and --tl 3.40:1 on --bg; --blue-l 4.26:1 on navy used by breadcrumbs/cookie banner/CTA email on 20-26 pages), maximum-scale=1.0 on 3 pages, no <main> landmark on 23 of 26 public pages, a nav dropdown whose aria-expanded never updates on all 26 pages, a mobile menu with no Escape/focus-trap, and stat counters that ignore prefers-reduced-motion via a declared-but-never-used constant. DESIGN.md's "Contrast (verified)" table is materially false on 3 of 8 entries (claims 6.8:1 for a 4.71:1 pair and AA for a 2.05:1 pair). Positives verified: skip-link present with valid #main target on all 26 public pages, global :focus-visible with dark-section white-ring variant, comprehensive reduced-motion CSS in both systems, gated JS parallax/hero-rotate, clean aria-hidden usage (decorative only; Mailchimp honeypot correctly tabindex=-1), and genuinely viewport-aware ARIA in main.js (syncSvcAria via matchMedia 900px).

findings.title: maximum-scale=1.0 blocks pinch-zoom on 3 pages (banned pattern, WCAG 1.4.4)
findings.evidence: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0"> on index.html, quote-janitorial.html, quote-dayporter.html. quote.html correctly uses "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover" (no max-scale) and all other pages omit it — inconsistent. Likely motivated by sub-16px inputs (.footer-nl-form input is .84rem); legacy quote .fg inputs are already 1rem=16px so max-scale is unnecessary even there.
findings.recommendation: Remove maximum-scale=1.0 from all 3 pages; fix iOS zoom-on-focus by raising input font-size to 16px instead (see separate finding).

findings.title: System A token --tm #6B7A8D fails AA for normal text site-wide (4.23:1 on --bg, 4.01:1 on --cream)
findings.evidence: Computed: #6B7A8D on #FAFBFC = 4.23:1; on #F3F5F8 = 4.01:1 (AA normal text needs 4.5:1). 18 color:var(--tm) rules in css/styles.css, all small text: .ddi .ddsc (.72rem), .sec-stat (.9rem), .test-card-light .test-role, .legal-date (.85rem), .trust-rating-detail (.82rem), .trust-quote-role (.72rem), .article-content .article-meta (.85rem), .more-articles-card span (.75rem), .nm-header span (.7rem), .feat-test-role (.78rem), .ind-card-body p (.8rem), .featured-info .role (.8rem), +6 more.
findings.location: css/styles.css :root --tm; 18 selectors; affects all 26 public pages (nav dropdown descriptions + legal dates + article meta etc.)
findings.recommendation: Darken --tm to >=#5E6D80 (4.5:1 on #F3F5F8) or switch small-text usages to --tb #495568 (7.28:1).

findings.title: System A token --tl #7A8A9E fails AA at 3.40:1, used for visible text and placeholders
findings.evidence: Computed: #7A8A9E on #FAFBFC = 3.40:1, on #F3F5F8 = 3.23:1. Usages in css/styles.css: .testimonial-title{font-size:.8rem;color:var(--tl)} (visible text, testimonials.html) and .form-input::placeholder{color:var(--tl)} (careers.html form).
findings.recommendation: Replace --tl with --tb or a >=4.5:1 value for .testimonial-title; placeholders should reach at least 4.5:1 since they carry format hints.

findings.title: --blue-l #4A82C7 on navy = 4.26:1 fails AA for small text in breadcrumbs (20 pages), cookie banner (26 pages), CTA email (7 pages)
findings.evidence: Computed: #4A82C7 on #0B1D38 = 4.26:1 (needs 4.5:1; all usages are .72-.85rem). Rules: .breadcrumb a{color:var(--blue-l)} inside .breadcrumb{font-size:.75rem} on navy .page-hero (20 pages contain class="breadcrumb"); .cookie-banner a{color:var(--blue-l);text-decoration:underline} on .cookie-banner{background:var(--navy)} with p font-size .8rem/.72rem (cookie-consent.js loaded by 26 pages); .cta-email a{color:var(--blue-l)} at .85rem on navy cta-banner (7 pages).
findings.location: css/styles.css .breadcrumb a, .cookie-banner a, .cta-email a; 20/26/7 pages respectively
findings.recommendation: Lighten link color on navy surfaces to >=#5b90d2 (4.5:1) or reuse --safe-text #CBD6E1 with underline for affordance.

findings.title: <main> landmark missing on 23 of 26 public pages; skip target is a <section>
findings.evidence: grep census: <main> count = 1 only on index.html, quote.html, quote-janitorial.html, quote-dayporter.html. All other pages (about, services, janitorial, day-porter, why-ecco, testimonials, careers, sustainability, blog, accessibility, privacy, terms, sitemap, 404, all 7 blog/ posts) have 0 <main>; their skip-link points to <section class="page-hero" id="main"> (e.g. about.html, blog.html, 404.html, privacy.html). Skip link itself works (href="#main", target exists, .skip-link{top:-100%}/.skip-link:focus{top:.5rem}), but there is no main landmark and the target wraps only the hero, not the page content. <header> element count is also 0 on all marketing pages (bare <nav>).
findings.location: all public *.html except index/quote/quote-janitorial/quote-dayporter; skip-link markup identical across pages
findings.recommendation: Wrap page content after nav in <main id="main"> on the 23 pages (move id off the hero section); optionally wrap nav in <header>.

findings.title: DESIGN.md 'Contrast (verified)' table is false on 3 of 8 entries — one claimed-AA pair is actually 2.05:1
findings.evidence: DESIGN.md:42-46,65-69 vs computed with actual tokens from css/quote-flow.source.css: 'Sage on cream: 6.8:1 (AAA)' → #2D7A32 on #EEF2ED = 4.71:1 (AA only). 'White on sage: 6.4:1 (AAA)' → #FFF on #2D7A32 = 5.33:1 (AA only). 'Marfil on sage (active state): 4.1:1 (AA)' → #EFE8D7 on #6FB376 = 2.05:1 (fails everything; implementation is saved only because the dark selected-chip rule actually uses color:#0F1A20 on sage ≈ 6.4:1, quote-flow.source.css ~line 13193). 'Muted on cream: 4.6:1' → actual light --qf2-muted is #4F5C6E = 6.01:1 (doc understates; also note brief/docs cite #6B7A8D=3.87:1 which is NOT the shipped token). Dark claims slightly overstated but passing: sage 6.06 (claimed 6.5/6.8), ink 12.42 (claimed 13.8), muted 5.27 (claimed 5.2 OK).
findings.location: DESIGN.md lines 42-46 and 65-69; tokens at css/quote-flow.source.css (--qf2-muted:#4F5C6E light / #8A9AAB dark; dark chip rule ~13186-13197)
findings.recommendation: Recompute and correct the DESIGN.md table; record that marfil-on-sage is forbidden as a text pair and that #0F1A20-on-sage is the canonical active-state pair.

findings.title: Nav Services dropdown: aria-expanded never updates, role=menu without menu semantics — on all 26 pages
findings.evidence: Trigger: <a href="services.html" class="nl" aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded="false"> with popup <div class="ddp" role="menu"> containing role="menuitem" links, duplicated on every page (grep role="menu" → 26 files). Dropdown opens via CSS only: .ndd:focus-within .ddp,.ndd:hover .ddp{opacity:1;visibility:visible}. js/main.js contains zero 'ndd' references — aria-expanded stays "false" forever while the popup is open; no arrow-key navigation, no Escape, no aria-controls, violating the menu-role contract.
findings.location: nav block in all 26 public *.html; css/styles.css .ndd rules; js/main.js (no handler)
findings.recommendation: Either drop role=menu/menuitem (plain list of links + aria-expanded toggled in JS) or implement full menu keyboard pattern; sync aria-expanded on focus/hover open.

findings.title: Mobile nav menu: no Escape close, no focus trap while body scroll is locked
findings.evidence: js/main.js line 3: mobTog click toggles nm.classList 'active', sets aria-expanded (correct), sets document.body.style.overflow='hidden'. closeNav() only fires on link click or overlay click. The only keydown handlers in main.js are svc-tabs arrow keys (line ~98); grep shows no 'Escape' anywhere in main.js. Keyboard users cannot dismiss the menu without tabbing to a link, and focus can leave the open menu into the overlaid page.
findings.location: js/main.js IIFE line 3 (mobTog/closeNav), nav markup all pages
findings.recommendation: Add document keydown Escape → closeNav() + return focus to #mobTog; trap Tab within #navMenu while open (chat-widget.js already implements this exact pattern to copy).

findings.title: Stat counters ignore prefers-reduced-motion; gating constant declared but never used
findings.evidence: js/main.js line 3: animateCounter() runs a 1400ms rAF number animation on .stat-num with no motion check; statObs triggers it unconditionally. `const prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;` is declared at the end of line 3 and referenced nowhere else (grep: only the declaration). By contrast parallax (line 236) and hero-rotate (lines 330/378) return early correctly. Affected pages with .stat-num: index.html, about.html, testimonials.html (+index-reference.html).
findings.recommendation: Inside statObs (or animateCounter), if matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches set el.textContent to final value and return; delete or use the dead const.

findings.title: Chat widget toggle lacks aria-expanded/aria-controls; aria-modal=true dialog over non-inert page
findings.evidence: js/chat-widget.js line ~216: toggle button markup has aria-label="Ask Alina" but no aria-expanded/aria-controls; panel is role="dialog" aria-modal="true" while the page behind is never inert/aria-hidden (a manual Tab trap at lines 894-898 cycles [closeBtn, resetBtn, input, sendBtn] — note the in-panel CTA link #eccoChatCta and quick-reply buttons are not in that array, so Shift+Tab wrap skips them). Escape close + focus restore to toggle ARE implemented (lines 896-898). Loaded on 24 pages (all public pages except quote.html and dev artifacts).
findings.recommendation: Add aria-expanded sync + aria-controls="eccoChatPanel" on #eccoChatToggle; either drop aria-modal or compute the focusable list dynamically (panel.querySelectorAll) so CTA/quick replies are inside the trap.

findings.title: Chat widget hijacks Cmd/Ctrl+K globally on 24 pages
findings.evidence: js/chat-widget.js line ~894: document.addEventListener('keydown', ... if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'k') { e.preventDefault(); ...toggle chat... }) — overrides the browser/extension search shortcut on every page that loads the widget (24 pages), with no UI hint and no way to disable.
findings.recommendation: Remove the shortcut or scope it (e.g., only when chat already open / use a less-contended combo) and document it in the widget tooltip.

findings.title: Touch targets below project 44px minimum: footer social 36px, cookie buttons ~31px, desktop nav links ~32px, chat tooltip close ~21px
findings.evidence: .footer-social a{width:36px;height:36px} (all pages with footer). .cookie-btn{padding:.5rem 1.2rem;font-size:.8rem} ≈ 31px tall (26 pages). Desktop .nl{padding:.5rem 1.1rem;font-size:.82rem} ≈ 32px tall (mobile variant correctly gets min-height:48px). chat-widget.js .tip-close{font-size:.9rem;padding:.2rem} ≈ 21x21px — this one also fails WCAG 2.2 AA 2.5.8 (24px min). Compliant counter-examples exist in the same sheet: .btt 44x44, .nm-social a 44x44, .footer-legal a min-height:44px, .opt/.day-btn/.time-chip min-height:44/48px — so the rule is applied inconsistently.
findings.recommendation: min-width/min-height:44px + inline-flex per project convention; .tip-close is the priority (hard WCAG 2.5.8 failure).

findings.title: Form inputs below 16px trigger iOS zoom-on-focus (the very bug max-scale=1 was masking)
findings.evidence: .form-input,.form-select,.form-textarea{font-size:.88rem} = 14.1px (careers.html application form, pages without max-scale → iOS zooms). .footer-nl-form input[type=email]{font-size:.84rem} = 13.4px (footer on all pages). Quote V2 does it right: css/quote-flow.source.css line 11933 `.qf2-field input, .qf2-field textarea { font-size: 16px; ... }` and line 12519 `.qf-dp-time-input { font-size: 16px }`; legacy .fg input is 1rem=16px.
findings.location: css/styles.css .form-input/.form-select/.form-textarea, .footer-nl-form input[type=email]; affects careers.html + footer newsletter on all 26 pages
findings.recommendation: Raise both rules to 16px (at least within a max-width media query), then remove maximum-scale=1.0 from index.html.

findings.title: Legacy quote wizard: low-alpha text/borders over a 72%-opacity photo overlay — worst-case contrast below AA
findings.evidence: quote-janitorial.html/quote-dayporter.html render chips over <div class="wiz-bg" style="background-image:url('images/stock/hero-office.webp')"> (opacity:.18 over #FAFBFC body) + .wiz-overlay{background:linear-gradient(180deg,rgba(11,29,56,.88),rgba(11,29,56,.72))}. At the 72% bottom stop over a mid-bright photo region, effective bg ≈ #485569; .time-chip{color:rgba(255,255,255,.65);font-size:.82rem} computes to ≈3.9:1 (needs 4.5). Placeholders rgba(255,255,255,.35) ≈ 2.68:1 over input bg. Chip borders rgba(255,255,255,.12) ≈ 1.25:1 vs 3:1 non-text minimum where border is the only component boundary. Focus outline rgba(255,255,255,.4) = 3.72:1 passes 3:1. Same-component text at .85 alpha (10.9:1) and .7 alpha (7.7:1) pass.
findings.recommendation: Deepen overlay bottom stop to >=.85 or lift .time-chip text to rgba(255,255,255,.8)+ and borders to .25+; raise placeholder alpha to ~.6.

findings.title: --green-l #3D9A43 used as small text on light surfaces fails AA (3.43-3.56:1)
findings.evidence: Computed: #3D9A43 on #FAFBFC = 3.43:1, on #FFF = 3.56:1. Failing usages in css/styles.css: .nm-contact a{color:var(--green-l);font-size:.88rem;font-weight:600} (mobile menu, white panel, all 26 pages); .sec-lbl-l{color:var(--green-l);background:rgba(61,154,67,.12)} small uppercase labels; .wiz-sec.done{color:var(--green-l)} (legacy quote progress, though over dark overlay there = 4.7:1 OK). On navy, green-l = 4.73:1 (passes for its uses). Solid --green #2D7A32 on light = 5.14:1 is the safe alternative.
findings.location: css/styles.css .nm-contact a, .sec-lbl-l; all pages (nav menu), section labels on light sections
findings.recommendation: Use --green #2D7A32 for green text on light backgrounds; reserve --green-l for dark surfaces.

findings.title: Newsletter and careers forms have no error announcement pattern (vs quote V2 which has full a11y plumbing)
findings.evidence: footer-nl-form (Mailchimp, 26 pages): input has aria-label="Email for newsletter" + required, but no aria-live region, posts target="_blank" (new window without warning); honeypot correctly aria-hidden+tabindex=-1. careers.html form (formsubmit.co): labels + required + aria-required present, but grep aria-live|role="alert" = 0. Contrast: js/quote-flow.js implements role="alert" aria-live="assertive" toast (line ~23), role="status" offline banner, aria-invalid + appended aria-describedby tokens (lines 1620-1658), inert on completed screens (line 1107).
findings.location: index.html + all pages (footer-nl-form); careers.html #apply form; reference implementation js/quote-flow.js
findings.recommendation: Acceptable to rely on native validation short-term; add a visually-hidden aria-live="polite" status node for submit feedback and a 'opens in new window' hint on the newsletter button.

findings.title: Decorative background div given role=img and accessible name
findings.evidence: quote-janitorial.html (and quote-dayporter.html): <div class="wiz-bg" style="background-image:url('images/stock/hero-office.webp')" role="img" aria-label="Office background"> — purely decorative backdrop announced to screen readers; also an inline style (violates project zero-inline-styles rule).
findings.recommendation: Replace with aria-hidden="true", drop role/aria-label, move background-image to a CSS class.

findings.title: Publicly deployed dev artifacts have no a11y scaffolding; mobile-test.html lacks viewport meta entirely
findings.evidence: Census: mockups.html, font-compare.html, font-specimen.html, color-swatches.html, mobile-test.html, emailjs-template.html all have skip=0 main=0 header=0 footer=0 nav=0; index-reference.html has no skip-link/main; mobile-test.html has NO <meta name="viewport"> at all; font-*/color-swatches use a different viewport string (width=device-width,initial-scale=1) than the site norm.
findings.location: mockups.html, font-compare.html, font-specimen.html, color-swatches.html, index-reference.html, quote.backup.html, mobile-test.html, emailjs-template.html (repo root, deployed)
findings.recommendation: Exclude dev artifacts from deploy (Cloudflare _headers noindex at minimum, ideally remove); if kept, they inherit none of the site's a11y baseline.

findings.title: Chat panel focus-trap array omits in-panel CTA link and quick replies; messages region OK
findings.evidence: js/chat-widget.js Tab handler builds f=[closeBtn, resetBtn, input, sendBtn] — #eccoChatCta (a link rendered between header and messages) and dynamically added quick-reply buttons are tabbable but sit outside first/last wrap logic, so Shift+Tab from closeBtn jumps to sendBtn skipping them at the wrap point. role="log" aria-live="polite" aria-relevant="additions" on messages is correct.
findings.recommendation: Compute focusables via panel.querySelectorAll('a[href],button:not([disabled]),textarea') at keydown time.

findings.title: Hero gradient-text reduced-motion fallback lands at exactly 3.16:1 (large-text pass, no margin)
findings.evidence: styles.css: .hero h1 em{background:linear-gradient(90deg,#8fd48c,#5ba8e6,#6bd568,#8fd48c);-webkit-text-fill-color:transparent;animation:shimmer 4s linear infinite} — animated gradient stops are all light (OK on navy). Reduced-motion override sets -webkit-text-fill-color:var(--green) #2D7A32 on navy #0B1D38 = 3.16:1 — passes 3:1 only because hero h1 is large text; green-l #3D9A43 (4.73:1) would match the static mobile variant .hero-ttl-desktop em which already uses var(--green-l). Gradient text itself also violates PRODUCT.md 'no gradient text'.
findings.location: css/styles.css .hero h1 em + prefers-reduced-motion block; index.html hero
findings.recommendation: Use --green-l in the reduced-motion fallback for consistency and margin.

findings.title: Nav scroll-progress gradient bar passes non-text contrast (verified, no action)
findings.evidence: .nav::after{height:2px;background:linear-gradient(90deg,var(--green),var(--blue));width:var(--scroll-progress,0%)} on .nav background rgba(250,251,252,.92): #2D7A32 = 5.14:1, #3068AD = 5.46:1 vs adjacent — both exceed the 3:1 non-text minimum. Recorded so synthesis doesn't re-flag; the 2px height is a perceivability (not contrast) limitation.
findings.recommendation: None required.

reports.inventory: CONTRAST CENSUS (computed WCAG ratios). System A on --bg #FAFBFC: --tb #495568=7.28 PASS; --tm #6B7A8D=4.23 FAIL-normal (4.01 on cream #F3F5F8); --tl #7A8A9E=3.40 FAIL; --green #2D7A32=5.14 PASS; --green-l #3D9A43=3.43 FAIL-normal; --blue #3068AD=5.46 PASS; --blue-l #4A82C7=3.82 FAIL-normal; --red #C84444=4.64 PASS. On navy #0B1D38: --safe-text #CBD6E1=11.42; --twm #C8D5E2=11.28; footer-legal (twm@.6 opacity)=4.85 marginal-pass; --blue-l=4.26 FAIL-normal; --green-l=4.73 PASS; --green=3.16 (large only). White on: --blue=5.66, --green=5.33, --green-l=3.56 FAIL-normal, --blue-l=3.96 FAIL-normal, --red=4.81. System B light (#EEF2ED): ink #0B1D38=14.88; muted #4F5C6E=6.01 (NOT #6B7A8D=3.87 as docs imply); sage #2D7A32=4.71; error #B23B3B=5.18. System B dark (#1B2733): ink #EFE8D7=12.42; muted #8A9AAB=5.27; sage #6FB376=6.06; chip-active #0F1A20-on-sage≈6.4 PASS; marfil-on-sage=2.05 FAIL (doc claims 4.1 AA). Third token set --stage-* in styles.css (quote conversational): muted #5A6B7F on #F5F1EA=4.85 PASS; sage on stage-cream=4.73 PASS. PAGE CENSUS (26 public pages): skip-link 26/26 present, target #main exists everywhere; <main> only 4/26 (index, quote, quote-janitorial, quote-dayporter); <header> 0/26 marketing (quote.html's 14 are card headers); maximum-scale=1.0 on 3 pages; role=menu nav dropdown on 26 pages with frozen aria-expanded; chat-widget.js on 24 pages; cookie-consent on 26; breadcrumb on 20; stats-bar counters on 3 (+index-reference). Reduced-motion: styles.css global block + 16 component blocks; quote-flow.source.css 39 blocks; JS gated: parallax(236), hero-rotate(378), quote-flow(394/906/4904); NOT gated: animateCounter (dead const prefersReducedMotion). aria-hidden: 268 occurrences sampled across 6 pages — all decorative (icons, separators, honeypot with tabindex=-1); zero on focusable content. matchMedia-aware ARIA: implemented (syncSvcAria, 900px) in main.js; quote-flow uses inert+aria-hidden pairing on done screens.
